races.name: Ryan's Cleaning Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Newbrook Diamond
runners.spotlight: Point winner at Oldtown a year ago; useful form in maiden hurdles, though concerning that he has not progressed since second to the Albert Bartlett runner-up Fruit De Mer at Naas on the first attempt; has shown a tendency to hang in two races at Punchestown; blinkers may help.
runners.comment: Has been running consistently without showing any progress, should not be far away

runners.name: Colcannon
runners.spotlight: Top bumper performer; won Leopardstown Grade 2 (2m, soft) in February last year; good third behind Bambino Fever at the Punchestown festival next time; beaten 9l by Koktail Brut (now rated 139) at Punchestown on hurdling debut when Splendid Fellow was a remote third; has failed to match that form in outings at Navan and Naas.
runners.comment: Top bumper performer last season, has failed to reach the same level of form over hurdles

runners.name: Splendid Fellow
runners.spotlight: Third in a point-to-point at Dromahane last April; respectable fourth on hurdling/stable debut at Limerick; did best of the remainder in a Punchestown maiden dominated by the subsequent Royal Bond winner Koktail Brut and Colcannon; form of his Cork second to subsequent Grade 2 winner and Grade 1 second Kazanksy suggests he may cope with Colcannon now.
runners.comment: Absent since second at Cork in December, form has been advertised by the winner Kazansky

runners.name: Mossy Way
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in bumpers last season for Pat Doyle; started this season well with an emphatic bumper win at Limerick in November on heavy; kept good company in two races in winners' bumpers for this stable (last of seven in Grade 2 at DRF); well held by Newbrook Diamond on the form of hurdles debut at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Bumper winner,  well held by Newbrook Diamond on the form of hurdles debut at Punchestown
runners.owner: Anthony Vaughan & D P Gaffney & James J Gaffney

runners.name: Brave Brigadier
runners.spotlight: Ran three times for Tom Gibney last season; twice second at Punchestown, including behind Kappa Jy Pyke, a leading fancy for today's Grade 1 novice chase; failed to improve at Navan on his third start; interesting as a rare jumps runner for Natalia Lupini. entitled to need the run.
runners.comment: Showed promise with two Punchestown seconds last year, has changed stable, any need this

runners.name: Brother Walfrid
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m4f hurdle winner, plenty of stamina in the family; runner-up on the second of two point-to-point starts;
runners.comment: Plenty of stamina in the family; runner-up on the second of two point-to-point starts;

runners.name: Claude Du Val
runners.spotlight: Finished in front of the talented Gaucher and smart Flat performer Ascending when third in a maiden hurdle at Navan two seasons ago; getting on in years now and faces a stiff task after such a long absence, but worth a look for future reference,
runners.comment: Of some interest on the form of his only start two seasons ago, stiff task after 784 days

runners.name: Blue Du Neuf
runners.spotlight: Recent Navan fifth was a step in the right direction; had plenty of horses behind but was beaten in excess of 40l; further improvement needed.
runners.comment: Navan fifth was a step in the right direction but was beaten in excess of 40l, more needed

runners.name: Thiscouldbetheone
runners.spotlight: Brother to stable's Grade 1 novice hurdle winner City Island; far fifth on debut in a Listed Limerick bumper last season, and ran to a similar level at the Punchestown festival when last seen; kept on for sixth in a 25-runner maiden hurdle at Naas in January; 93
runners.comment: Held by runner-up Newbrook Diamond on the evidence of a Naas run

runners.name: Chopsticks
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 22 starts on the Flat but placed eight times; behind when unseated at the last on recent hurdling debut at Navan.
runners.comment: Winner and often placed on Flat at a modest level, beaten when unseating on hurdling debut

runners.name: Thatsmyroadie
runners.spotlight: Nothing to recommend after his first three maiden hurdle outings.
runners.comment: Nothing to recommend after his first three maiden hurdle outings

runners.name: Midfield Magic
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother by Poet's Word to winners; dam unraced half-sister to high-class hurdle/chase winner Weapons Amnesty.
runners.comment: Newcomer out of an unraced half-sister to high-class hurdle/chase winner Weapons Amnesty

runners.name: Deep Warrior
runners.spotlight: Dam runner-up in A bumper, closely related to the top-class Altior and the useful Princess Leya; need to improve considerably form lat month's Cork debut
runners.comment: Dam closely related to Altior, needs to improve considerably from last month's Cork debut

runners.name: God The Highness
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in staying handicaps at a modest level on the Flat; not a likely winner on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Placed twice at a modest level on the Flat, not a likely winner on hurdles debut

runners.name: Crystal Yeats
runners.spotlight: From a good jumping family; some merit in last season's form in points; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, from a good jumping family, some merit in last season's form in points

runners.name: Eben Zaabeel
runners.spotlight: Seven-race Flat maiden for Charlie Hills, rated 79; best form on AW, might need better ground, third reserve,
runners.comment: Third reserve, 79-rated Flat maiden, best form on AW, might need better ground

runners.name: Zarushio
runners.spotlight: Nothing in his three runs suggests he can be competitive in a maiden of this standard.
runners.comment: Nothing in his three runs suggests he can be competitive in a maiden of this standard.

runners.name: Codgers Rock
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort at a huge price on debut at Down Royal; made a bad mistake two from home and dropped away.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort on debut at Down Royal, made a bad mistake two from home and dropped away

runners.name: Old Saxony
runners.spotlight: Won at the eighth and final attempt on the Flat in 2024 for David Simcock in a 1m6f Southwell AW handicap last October; has made no impact in two races over hurdles in testing conditions.
runners.comment: AW winner in Britain, has made no impact in two races over hurdles in testing conditions

runners.name: Theabsolutegovnor
runners.spotlight: Fair run in a Galway festival bumper last year; has hinted at ability in two maiden hurdle outings; possibly one for handicaps in time.
runners.comment: Has hinted at ability in two maiden hurdle outings, possibly one for handicaps in time

runners.name: Herecomesmybaby
runners.spotlight: Remote fourth of five finishers in a maiden point; huge price when tailed off at Navan on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Remote fourth of five finishers in a maiden point, huge price when tailed off at Navan

runners.name: Clonbury Court
runners.spotlight: Unable to make any impact in outings at this venue, Naas and Navan; no apparent chance.
runners.comment: Unable to make any impact in outings at this venue, Naas and Navan; no apparent chance.

runners.name: Fun Daddy
runners.spotlight: Showed up well before fading into fifth behind the subsequent Albert Bartlett second Fruit De Mer at Naas in November; open to improvement but has plenty to find with runner-up Newbrook Diamond.
runners.comment: Open to improvement but has plenty to find with runner-up Newbrook Diamond on Naas form

runners.name: Il Pirata
runners.spotlight: Hints of promise in three maidens; more likely a handicap prospect; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, hints of promise in three maidens, more likely a handicap prospect

runners.name: Areema Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds when in rear in a 2m4f maiden at Down Royal on his only start.
runners.comment: Three-figure odds when in rear in a 2m4f maiden at Down Royal on his only start

races.name: Cawley Furniture Novice Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Doctor Elvis
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but progressing in each run, notably runner-up latest two in 2m3f maidens, at Naas and when chasing home a ready 8.5l winner at Limerick last month; cheekpieces now added for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Solid form in 2m3f maidens recently; cheekpieces now added for handicap debut

runners.name: Tir Og
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well earlier in the season with handicap wins at Sligo, Downpatrick and Naas; having risen 30lb in ratings, form has levelled out of late albeit recent Wexford third was a solid one; consistent mare but others may now be better handicapped.
runners.comment: Most progressive earlier in season but handicapper may have her measure now

runners.name: Sticwiththeprocess
runners.spotlight: Favourite when touched off in this race last year (off 105) and reappeared to land 2m maiden here in November on similarly testing ground; beaten favourite both starts since, latest when dropping out tamely before last in C&D rated novice in January; tongue-tie now added to cheekpieces worn the last twice.
runners.comment: Touched off in this last year and won maiden this term; questions to answer after latest

runners.name: Takenasred
runners.spotlight: Fair level of form without yet winning, placed in Down Royal maiden and Ayr novice; two most recent runs below that level however and beginning to look exposed.
runners.comment: Two most recent runs below best and beginning to look exposed

runners.name: Frankies Freebie
runners.spotlight: Won a point-to-point on good ground last May; coped with much softer ground on first three maiden hurdle runs when placed behind some decent types; recent Limerick run was poor though, forcing it as usual but dropping out tamely; others preferred on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Front-runner with solid form until poor Limerick effort last time; others preferred

runners.name: Diamond Tipp
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably on hurdles debut at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) last summer and some okay runs in handicaps afterwards; absent since October and probably wants better ground.
runners.comment: Absent since October and probably wants better ground

runners.name: Royal Soldier
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved to get off the mark on fourth attempt hurdling and on handicap debut at Gowran last month, coming late under patient ride to win going away over 2m (heavy): every chance he'll improve again over this longer trip and 9lb rise workable.
runners.comment: Good impression when scoring at Gowran; likely more to come upped in trip here

runners.name: Jaynemax
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best effort yet on latest when up with pace throughout in finishing second of 18 in 2m3f Leopardstown novice handicap last month (soft-to-heavy); not sure this slightly longer trip will suit.
runners.comment: Recent Leopardstown second; not sure this slightly longer trip will suit

runners.name: Mi Lucky Cailin
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner is 1-18 over hurdles with the victory coming off a mark of 100 in decisive fashion at Thurles (2m4f, heavy) in January; ran a nice race when fourth in a Listed novice at Navan last time (2m4f, heavy); don't rule out.
runners.comment: Good run in Navan Listed latest; don't rule out on overall profile

runners.name: Lauros Legend
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best run came when placed in good-ground Wexford maiden last summer; testing ground wouldn't have suited on last month's comeback run and may need more drying to be considered.
runners.comment: Better for recent comeback run but probably wants better ground

runners.name: Green Hope
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with shock 150-1 success in 2m1f Down Royal maiden in January (second and third both good winners since, rated 129 and 125 respectively); nowhere near that form following month however when third in 2m Ayr novice on soft; upped in trip for handicap debut and on a decent mark if taking that maiden win literally.
runners.comment: Down Royal maiden win looks good form; contender if he stays on handicap debut

runners.name: Sky And Sand
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on second handicap attempt, coming home well to assert from last over this trip at Gowran last month (heavy); 7lb rise but could go well.
runners.comment: Recent Gowran winner over this trip on heavy; 7lb rise but could go well

runners.name: Maverick Mack
runners.spotlight: Seemed to relish testing ground when scoring readily on hurdles debut in a Gowran maiden (2m, heavy) in November but later in month faded tamely in C&D novice; back from a break with stamina concerns.
runners.comment: Absent since tame C&D effort in November; others preferred

runners.name: Sheer Raz
runners.spotlight: Last month's Naas comeback run (10.5l fifth behind Umpire's Call) was a step in the right direction but lots more required here in a more competitive handicap.
runners.comment: Naas comeback run encouraging but lots more required here

runners.name: Katie Daniels
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden in November on heavy but hasn't gone from that, most recently weakening two out in 2m7f Punchestown handicap in February; return to this trip should suit and tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner didn't look to stay latest; return to 2m4f to suit and tongue-tied now

runners.name: Zam Bobbler
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo was having only third start when landing ordinary 2m Wexford maiden last month, coming home strongly to beat a joint-favourite (pair clear); longer trip should suit but this a stiff task.
runners.comment: Wexford maiden winner for whom longer trip should suit but this a stiff task
runners.owner: Paul Dillon/Pearse Mehigan/Michael Hannon/P J Rothwell

runners.name: Some Song
runners.spotlight: Won Ballysteen point-to-point (yielding) for Pierce Power and fortunate winner when left in the lead at the last on hurdles debut at Down Royal (2m6f) in October; softer ground possibly against him when pulled up in Thurles rated novice in December and back from a break here.
runners.comment: Absent since pulled up at Thurles in December; may want better ground

runners.name: Poets Gale
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in a Galway bumper on debut but hasn't kicked on since then, well held in three maiden hurdles; opening mark looks plenty high on evidence to-date.
runners.comment: Well held in three maiden; opening mark looks plenty high on that evidence

runners.name: Umpires Call
runners.spotlight: Improved round of jumping to get off the mark last month in 2m3f Naas handicap, making most and rallying well from last (soft-to-heavy); 7lb rise so needs to step up again.
runners.comment: Showed good attitude for recent Naas win; 7lb rise so needs to step up again

runners.name: Don Jefe
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo stepped up nicely on latest of three maiden runs to win over C&D in early February, seeing it out strongly in admittedly a weak affair; type to improve again but will need to off this mark.
runners.comment: Won weak C&D maiden in February; much more on his plate here but can go well

races.name: Donohue Marquees Novice Hurdle (Grade 2)

runners.name: Koktail Brut
runners.spotlight: Has failed to make an effective transition to Grade 1 company in three attempts since in Grade 2 Royal Bond C&D victory over Blake in November; around 2l behind fellow outsider Too Bossy For Us (sixth) when eighth in the Supreme Novices'.
runners.comment: Limitations evident in Grade 1 races since C&D Royal Bond win, decent chance at this level

runners.name: Blake
runners.spotlight: Three Flat turf wins, including a Leopardstown premier handicap on heavy; has taken well to hurdling, winning Down Royal maiden and just denied by Koktail Brut in Grade 2 Royal Bond over C&D; 2l behind Koktail Brut when fifth in DRF Grade 1; sloppy jumping proved costly when beaten a neck by Le Labo at Navan; first time with cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Beaten a head by Koktail Brut in the Royal Bond, lost to Le Labo on latest, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Leader dAllier
runners.spotlight: Won five of his six starts in France, signing off with a 1m4f Grade 3 AQPS won at Vichy last summer; beaten favourite at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting; made all for an easy success at Punchestown in January; withdrawn from the Supreme Novices, reported lame.
runners.comment: 5-6 in AQPS races in France, limited hurdling experience but commands obvious respect

runners.name: Too Bossy For Us
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat handicapper; finished in mid-field in the 2025 Triumph on hurdling debut; easy winner of a Punchestown maiden in January; second-best of a disappointing Irish contingent when sixth in the Supreme Novices; giving him a slight edge over Koktail Brut.
runners.comment: Second-best of a below-par Irish contingent when sixth in the Supreme Novices

runners.name: Heads Up
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Bambino Fever in last year's Champion Bumper; won on hurdle debut at Listowel (2m, good-to-yielding) before 3l second to subsequent Challow Hurdle winner No Drama This End in Grade 2 at Cheltenham (2m5f, soft); much too free and jumped untidily in a similar Cheltenham race in January; back to form in winning a four-runner Punchestown event.
runners.comment: Smart performer in bumpers; good overall hurdles form except for one run when too keen

runners.name: Murat
runners.spotlight: Won bumpers at Killarney and Galway, almost a year apart, making all on latter occasion; beaten 17l by a smart horse at Naas on hurdling debut; took advantage of a mishap to El Cairos to win a maiden at Leopardstown; probably unsuited by soft ground when running poorly at Thurles two months ago.
runners.comment: Probably unsuited by soft ground when running poorly at Thurles, up against it here

runners.name: Ebony King
runners.spotlight: Made most to win Tipperary bumper on debut, beating subsequent winners; won on hurdles debut at the Galway festival; headed before last when falling at Kilbeggan; warmed up for a return to hurdling with a good run on AW at Dundalk; not suited by the race conditions
runners.comment: Maiden winner, should be fit after a good AW run, not suited by the race conditions

races.name: Paddy Kehoe Suspended Ceilings Novice Hurdle (Grade 2)

runners.name: Love Me Tender
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper, maiden hurdle (2m3f) and 2m Grade 3 novice without being extended, all on good-to-yielding; has acquitted creditably in Grade 1 Leopardstown novices' since, none too fluent when third at Christmas (heavy) and keen enough when third over 2m6f behind a stablemate at the DRF; drop back to 2m4f probably in his favour and open to improvement on better ground.
runners.comment: Creditable Grade 1 placings of late; may do better again on quicker surface

runners.name: Lazare de Star
runners.spotlight: Successful on second attempt hurdling in 2m5f Leopardstown maiden at Christmas and better form (albeit beaten at odds-on each time) when runner-up in 2m novices at Naas and Punchestown, latter occasion in first-time hood when clear of remainder when going down by a head on heavy; definite contender again but more needed and return to 2m4f to suit.
runners.comment: Beaten odds-on shot last twice; return to 2m4f may suit

runners.name: Generous Risk
runners.spotlight: Won point-to-point for Donnchadh Doyle last March before selling for Â£250,000; placed in three bumpers (including here) before successful hurdles debut last month in 2m5f Leopardstown maiden, making most and going clear from last (soft); type to improve and don't rule out.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden winner almost certainly capable of better; respected

runners.name: I Started A Joke
runners.spotlight: Won 2m Punchestown maiden hurdle in good style in January (soft-to-heavy) and excellent run following month when favourite in very competitive 19-runner 2m Leopardstown handicap, going clear with winner from last; cheekpieces added and upped in trip on switching to novice company.
runners.comment: Handicap second; cheekpieces added and upped in trip on switching to novice company

runners.name: Kielys Place
runners.spotlight: Bandon point winner a year ago for Eugene O'Sullivan and made fine impression on return for top yard in February, making all to beat an odds-on stablemate readily in 2m4f Clonmel maiden (heavy), jumping well; nice prospect, could make an impact in Graded company here.
runners.comment: Fine impression when making all at Clonmel; could make an impact in Graded company here

runners.name: Hes On Fire
runners.spotlight: Went close over hurdles at Auteuil (2m2f, heavy) in November on third and final start in France; landed long odds on yard debut in 2m4f Carlisle maiden in February in a canter (second won next time); could be anything and Townend's pick.
runners.comment: Carlisle win franked since; could be anything and Townend's pick

runners.name: Ksar Fatal
runners.spotlight: Gowran bumper winner last season for Willie Mullins; reappeared for current yard in February to lower the colours of a Mullins-trained odds-on shot in 2m4f Punchestown maiden hurdle, that runner-up subsequently a good winner at Limerick; exciting prospect, one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Punchestown debut hurdles win franked by the runner-up since; exciting prospect

runners.name: Hipop De Loire
runners.spotlight: Classy sort on the Flat, third in last year's Doncaster Cup; 9yo has had just three runs over hurdles, winning Galway maiden last summer, most recently returned from five months' absence when pulled up after never featuring in Grade 1 Albert Bartlett at Cheltenham; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Classy Flat 9yo; pulled up in Albert Bartlett; cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Royal Gladiator
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on sole bumper start (Navan) and having finished fourth to Lazare De Star on hurdles debut at Leopardstown over Christmas, returned from a break to win over same trip at Cork last month, showing good attitude from the last (yielding); more needed.
runners.comment: Good attitude for Cork maiden win at this trip; more needed

runners.name: Saint Baco
runners.spotlight: French recruit landed odds on yard debut when beating Lazare De Star in 2m Navan maiden in December; stiff tasks since in Grade 1 novices' over further, dropping away tamely at Naas and on the retreat when last-flight faller in Turners at Cheltenham; easier 2m4f should suit.
runners.comment: Grade 1 efforts disappointing but down in grade here and easier 2m4f to suit

runners.name: You Proof
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo finished second in a point-to-point, just fair rules debut effort in bumper here in November 2024 and absent until winning 2m6f Limerick maiden hurdle at Christmas, travelling well and picking up well to beat an odds-on shot; Danny Mullins now switches to Saint Baco.
runners.comment: Good impression over 2m6f at Limerick but Danny Mullins now switches to Saint Baco

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Honeysuckle Mares Novice Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Oldschool Outlaw
runners.spotlight: Progressed really well since joining the Elliott yard this term, winning Navan Listed bumper and first two attempts hurdling, notably 9l defeat of Place De La Nation in 2m3f Grade 3 mares' novice here; better again in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham, staying on second of 22 with several of these behind, notably Place De La Nation and Charme De Faust; return to this longer trip to suit.
runners.comment: Ryanair second should appreciate return to longer trip; big player

runners.name: Hows Hannah
runners.spotlight: Made good impression in winning first two starts at Punchestown; mares bumper and value for much more in 2m7f mares maiden hurdle; better again when a clear second to more experienced Zanoosh in Grade 3 Shannon Spray Novices at Limerick over 2m7f; tongue-tie now fitted and potential for more but drop in trip may not be ideal and 3lb worse off with Zanoosh.
runners.comment: Progressive mare but may not overturn Limerick form with Zanoosh on 3lb worse terms

runners.name: Zanoosh
runners.spotlight: Rapidly improving mare is seeking a five-timer following four heavy-ground wins, most recently outstaying How's Hannah over 2m7f in Grade 3 Shannon Spray Novices' at Limerick last month; equally effective at 2m4f and 3lb better off with that rival here; major chance.
runners.comment: Rapidly improving mare is seeking a five-timer; loves it heavy; major chance

runners.name: A Path To Ronda
runners.spotlight: Was gaining second success over hurdles in lowly 2m Kelso mares novice last time; previously ran to much better level of form when placed in Listed/Grade 2 mares' contests; this looks a stiff task though.
runners.comment: Decent form in mares' contests in Britain but this looks much deeper

runners.name: Charme De Faust
runners.spotlight: Sole 4yo in the field, having won Thurles maiden before fine run for one so inexperienced when fourth of 22 in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham, effort just flattening out on run-in; interesting upped in trip here with obvious scope for improvement and Townend's pick.
runners.comment: Ryanair fourth on only third start; obvious scope for improvement and Townend's pick

runners.name: Place De La Nation
runners.spotlight: Second-season hurdler won 2m maiden here in January before chasing home 9l winner Oldschool Outlaw in 2m3f Grade 3 mares' novice; got a lot closer to that rival in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham, only losing second on run-in to that rival; not sure this longer trip will play to her strengths but respected.
runners.comment: Only lost 2nd late on to Oldschool Outlaw in Ryanair; not sure this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Kingston Queen
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins have been at 2m3f, notably Warwick Grade 2 defeat of subsequent Ryanair Mares winner White Noise; keen in first-time cheekpieces in Ryanair and didn't get home when eighth of 22 behind same rival; headgear left off.
runners.comment: Too keen in cheekpieces in Ryanair; headgear now left off

runners.name: Amen Kate
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins include 2m Thurles mares novice in December; ran better than position suggests in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham latest, slowly away and bit short of room before last; lots to find however.
runners.comment: Okay Cheltenham effort in the circumstances but still looks to have plenty to find
runners.owner: Ciaran John Mooney & John Francis Ryan

runners.name: Future Prospect
runners.spotlight: Won bumper here last season and came good over hurdles in 2m Naas maiden in January (yielding); ran no sort of race however (pulled up) in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham and looks to have a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Ran no sort of race in Ryanair Mares' Novice, looks to have a bit to prove now

runners.name: Jackie Hobbs
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner; 2-4 over hurdles and much the best effort on latest in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham when staying on fifth; bit to find with several of these on that run however.
runners.comment: Ryanair fifth a career-best but she had three of today's rivals ahead of her

runners.name: Blue Velvet
runners.spotlight: Punchestown maiden winner at slightly shorter (heavy); another for whom things didn't go her way in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham but yard look the have stronger contenders here.
runners.comment: Things didn't go her way in Ryanair Mares' Novice; yard look the have stronger contenders

races.name: BOYLE Sports Novice Handicap Chase (Listed Race)

runners.name: Jigoro
runners.spotlight: Improved since going handicapping, decent Thurles effort followed by 2m Gowran win last month on heavy under a positive ride from today's rider; 5lb higher and this looks a much stronger race.
runners.comment: Recent Gowran winner under today's rider; 5lb higher and this looks a much stronger race

runners.name: Jalila Moriviere
runners.spotlight: 135-rated hurdler trounced weak opposition on chase debut at Sligo in October; much stiffer tasks since in Graded novices', staying on remote third over 2m at Cork and last month when made plenty of use of at Thurles but didn't seem to get home when third over 2m6f (soft); return to 2m may suit on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Didn't seem to stay 2m6f last time; entitled to improve and drop in trip may suit

runners.name: Anotherway
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; off the mark chasing in 2m1f Down Royal novice in January but found wanting on handicap debut behind Jigoro at Gowran on heavy, never landing a blow; opposable.
runners.comment: Found wanting on handicap debut behind Jigoro at Gowran on heavy; opposable

runners.name: Moudan
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner is proving a better chaser, winning 2m2f Limerick novice and most recently career-best in landing 2m1f Navan handicap last month (soft-to-heavy) under today's rider; bit to find with Jigoro on earlier Gowran running but progressing and can be involved off 5lb higher.
runners.comment: Recent Navan winner progressing nicely and can be involved off 5lb higher

runners.name: Rockbrook
runners.spotlight: Front-runner won Tramore novice on New Year's Day and followed up later in month under similar tactic in 2m Naas handicap (both 2m, soft); jumping let him down at Thurles following month over a bit further but don't rule out him bouncing back and this easy 2m to suit.
runners.comment: Jumping let him down at Thurles but don't rule out him bouncing back; this easy 2m to suit

runners.name: Karamoja
runners.spotlight: Placed in Grade 1 mares novice' on this card last year and off the mark over fences on latest of three attempts, making all for unextended win over 2m2f at Thurles last month (soft), jumping much better; still learning and could improve to play a role here; Townend's choice.
runners.comment: All-the-way Thurles winner; type to keep improving and could play a role; Townend's pick

runners.name: Break My Soul
runners.spotlight: Chase debut win at Gowran Park (2m, heavy) in November and easily best of four attempts since on latest when fine third of 20 in 2m Grand Annual at Cheltenham last month on handicap debut (good); no easy task for top-weight but has to be respected.
runners.comment: Grand Annual third; no easy task for top-weight but has to be respected

runners.name: Shuffle The Deck
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins on good-to-yielding; safely held in decent beginners chases of late but Walsh's pick in first handicap.
runners.comment: Handicap chase debut and seemingly Walsh's pick; would appreciate some drying in ground

runners.name: Birdie Or Bust
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler, two wins and third in 2024 Cheltenham Mares' Novices Hurdle; took a while to find her form over fences but won at Killarney and Listowel last year, absent since decent second in Grade 3 Punchestown novice in October; has run well fresh so respected on return although trip on the short side.
runners.comment: Has run well fresh so respected on return although trip on the short side

runners.name: Come Walk With Me
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well in defeat over hurdles and pick of three efforts over fences on latest when chasing home an all-the-way winner in 2m1f Leopardstown beginners last month (yielding); worth a look on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Leopardstown second promising; worth a look on handicap debut

runners.name: Harvard Guy
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner rated 138 in that discipline; not too much to get excited about so far chasing, never counting last twice in handicaps over much further; down markedly in trip here off potentially favourable mark.
runners.comment: Rated 13lb lower than over hurdles but this trip looks on the short side

runners.name: More Coko
runners.spotlight: Standout run from seven attempts over fences when chasing home a stablemate in Grade 3 handicap over C&D in January from 4lb out of handicap proper (yielding); heavy ground probably against him when 10l off Jigoro at Gowran last time, will appreciate any drying.
runners.comment: Heavy ground probably against him at Gowran; earlier C&D second was good though

races.name: WillowWarm Gold Cup (Grade 1)

runners.name: Western Fold
runners.spotlight: Progressive last summer, winning the Galway Plate (extended 2m6f, good), and a defeat of subsequent Savills Chase winner Affordale Fury at Gowran Park (2m4f, yielding to soft) in October; second to Kaid d'Authie in an unsatisfactory four-runner DRF event; faded from three out in Brown Advisory
runners.comment: Galway Plate winner, decent chance in theory at these weights, doubts about Grade 1 form

runners.name: Jacobs Ladder
runners.spotlight: Grade 3 novice hurdle winner at Naas last season; off the mark over fences at third attempt, taking a 2m beginners' at this venue in first-time cheekpieces; split today's rivals Kappa JyPyke and Predators Gold in a Grade 3 event over a near-2m4f trip at Punchestown; won a DRF handicap and has since landed the odds in a 2m Grade 3 at Navan.
runners.comment: Split Kappa JyPyke and Predators Gold at Punchestown, progressive in winning twice since

runners.name: Kala Conti
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to fences this season, recording two wins (2m5f/2m1f) by an aggregate of about 25l, latest easily in Grade 2 at Cork (soft) where she beat the subsequent Arkle winner Kargese; however, no match for Sixmilebridge in the Scilly Isles at Sandown
runners.comment: Beat the subsequent Arkle winner Kargese at Cork, no match for Sixmilebridge at Sandown

runners.name: Sixmilebridge
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has finished first past the post in all six attempts over 2m4f (disqualified on one occasion due to a banned substance); beat Kala Conto with plenty to spare in the Grade 1 Scilly Isles at Sandown (soft), task simplified by the exit of the Mullins-trained Kitzbuhel.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three races over fences including the Grade 1 Scilly Isles, strong contender

runners.name: Pure Steel
runners.spotlight: Point and maiden hurdle winner; promising chase debut before clear-cut win in a Punchestown rated novice contest in December (2m, heavy), travelling and jumping well; made a mistake three out and dropped in 2m3f Grade 3 event won by Kappa Jy Pyke; got his season back on track with a 2m win at Punchestown; live chance for trainer who this in 2024.
runners.comment: Bounced back from below-par run behind Kappa Jy Pyke with a 2m Punchestown win

runners.name: Predators Gold
runners.spotlight: Smart novice hurdler in 2023-24 season before 19-month absence; returned to win a beginners' event at Navan in December, beating former Supreme Novices' winner Slade Steel, who has proved disappointing over fences; jumping was untidy when he was beaten a odds-on chance in the Punchestown race won stablemate Kappa Jy Pyke; same riding arrangements now.
runners.comment: Odds-on when third to stablemate Kappa Jy Pyke at Punchestown, same riding arrangements

runners.name: Fleur In The Park
runners.spotlight: Smart novice hurdler at up to 3m last season; made a solid chasing debut at Gowran over 2m4f; won over 2m at Wexford in October; went close in a 2m Grade 2 contest at Punchestown in November; well below his best over an extended 2m5f at Thurles last time; faces a stiff task at this level.
runners.comment: Best of four chase runs when second in a Grade 2, faces a stiff task at this level

runners.name: Jimmy Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Ran only once last season, winning the Coral Cup at Cheltenham when returning from a long absence; made an impressive chasing debut over a longer trip here in November; flopped in Limerick Grade 1, dropping away since after a mistake three out; out of contention when falling at the last in Grade 1 at the DRF.
runners.comment: Coral Cup winner, began chase career with a good course win, jumping lapses since then

runners.name: Kappa Jy Pyke
runners.spotlight: Found wanting at Grade 1 level twice after maiden hurdle win last season; much more accomplished as a chaser; jumped well when beating a long odds-on stablemate in Thurles beginners' event in November; had no trouble with a rise in class at Punchestown, beating Jacob's Ladder readily in a race in which Predators Gold and Pure Steel gave below-par displays.
runners.comment: Beat Jacob's Ladder in a Grade 3 in which Predators Gold and Pure Steel were below-par

races.name: Race Displays Joseph O'Reilly Hunters Chase

runners.name: Chemical Energy
runners.spotlight: High-class novice chaser in the 2022/23 season, when second in the National Hunt Chase at Cheltenham; ran well in the Galway Plate and Munster National in 2024; generally ineffective in cross-country races since then; back-to-back point wins in the autumn offer some hope; however, he was tailed off in the hunter chase at Cheltenham last month.
runners.comment: Back-to-back point wins last autumn offer some hope, tailed off at Cheltenham

runners.name: Hunters Yarn
runners.spotlight: Three bumper wins, once for this yard, twice for Willie Mullins; three wins over jumps for Mullins before losing his way; has prospered in points this term for his original trainer, winning three from four, the exception a Dromahane third when he made a few mistakes; no disgrace in losing out to Panda Boy at Naas; harder to overlook Gowran defeat
runners.comment: Twice second in hunter chases since finishing behind Wrappedupinmay in a point, respected

runners.name: Big Interest
runners.spotlight: Looked capable of becoming a major force in this category when beating top hunter chaser Its On The Line at Down Royal's Christmas fixture in 2024; disappointing overall since then, except for an 18l win in this race last April; pulled up in two of his three hunter chase outings this ter and unseated rider on only point appearance; risky.
runners.comment: Won this race last year, pulled up in two of his three hunter chase outings this term

runners.name: Asphalt Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Both hunter chases wins have come at Downpatrick; down the field at Down Royal in December on his only run in this sphere this  season; placed in three points since October but needs to raise his game to feature here.
runners.comment: Two hunter chase wins at Downpatrick, needs to step up on recent points form to feature

runners.name: Stuzzikini
runners.spotlight: Failed to prosper in top staying handicaps after winning the 2024 Troytown for Gordon Elliott; should show the benefit of a point-to-point run at Oldtown in February.
runners.comment: Should show the benefit of a point-to-point run, smart staying handicap chaser at his best

runners.name: Wrappedupinmay
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin point winner in 2023; useful hurdler for Paul Nicholls, sold cheaply after two failures over fences; beaten 8l by Hunters Yarn at Moig South on return to pointing; reversed form with the winner at Dromahane and won readily at Carrigarostig in January; intended runner at Cheltenham until withdrawn because of concerns about the ground; valuable 7lb off.
runners.comment: Relegated Hunters Yarn to third at Dromahane, fair chance he can uphold that form now

runners.name: City Chief
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning the 2023 Towton (3m Grade 2) at Wetherby for Nicky Henderson; tailed off at Down Royal in December on his hunter chase debut; has twice in points in the last 12 months; recent Kirkistown success gives him an edge over Now Where Or When.
runners.comment: Former Towton winner, tailed off at Down Royal in December on his hunter chase debut

runners.name: Five Star Getaway
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life when winning four races over fences for Christian Williams in 2021; has been running well enough in points but nothing to suggest he could win a race of this standard
runners.comment: Has been running well enough in points, nothing to suggest he could win a race like this

runners.name: Now Where Or When
runners.spotlight: Veteran has been competing quite effectively in points, this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Has been competing quite effectively in points, this looks too demanding

races.name: Dermot Casey Tree Care Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Mino Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on good to soft in France; rated 126 after four runs over hurdles; soundly beaten in the Fred Winter at Cheltenham off that mark on latest but his previous Punchestown second behind Too Bossy For Us was promising; Harry Cobden a notable booking and he holds leading claims.
runners.comment: French bumper winner, rated 126, leading chance on form of Punchestown second

runners.name: Hygge
runners.spotlight: Third in both starts in academy hurdles and filled the same position in a 4yo maiden at Navan (2m, heavy) on latest, though was beaten 25l; best effort came on good to yielding ground so could do better now; rated 116; place chance.
runners.comment: Third in two academy hurdles and in 4yo maiden latest, decent ground a plus, place chance

runners.name: Asthelightdeclines
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; 50-1 and was near the back and under pressure when brought down two out on debut at Naas 32 days ago; likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: 50-1 and in rear when brought down two out at Naas on debut, best watched for now

runners.name: Mirfaq
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; Motivator gelding; E27,000 3yo; third foal; dam French 1m-12.5f winner, half-sister to French 2m4f/2m6f cross-country chase winner Speed Victory, out of prolific French 1m1f-13.5f winner Taraison; in good hands and needs checking in the market.
runners.comment: Motivator gelding, dam French 1m-1m2f winner, in good hands, check market

runners.name: Bartholomew
runners.spotlight: Ability on the Flat and again on hurdling debut when a 6.75l fourth of 19 in a Leopardstown maiden (2m, yielding) in December; off since and could go close if the new tongue tie ekes out a bit more.
runners.comment: Ability on the Flat and on hurdles debut in Dec', tongue tie could eke out more, chance

runners.name: Breeze Of Tobruk
runners.spotlight: Ocovango filly; dam point winner/third in bumper, half-sister to 2m hurdle winner Shanroe Society, out of 9.4f-1m5f Flat and 2m hurdle winner; watching brief advised on debut.
runners.comment: Ocovango filly, dam placed in a bumper and won a point, watching brief advised

runners.name: Earthwatch
runners.spotlight: Flat winner for William Haggas achieved a peak rating of 81 in that sphere; soundly beaten on debut for this yard in a Dundalk claimer in December and is likely best watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Flat winner in Britain rated 81, beaten in AW claimer latest, likely best watched here

runners.name: Matcho dAgrostis
runners.spotlight: Brave Mansonnien gelding; dam French maiden (Flat/jumps), half-sister to useful French 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Echo D'Estruval; yard capable of readying one and won this in 2024 so check the market.
runners.comment: Brave Mansonnien gelding, dam a French maiden, yard can ready one, check market

runners.name: Take The Free
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding; brother to point/2m3f hurdle winner That's Nice; dam multiple AQPS Flat and useful 2m/2m2f hurdle winner, half-sister to winners Felicidad (2m4f hurdle/chase) and Ecossais (2m2f-3m2f chase); one of three home-bred debutants for yard; market can guide.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park gelding, dam bumper and hurdle winner, market can guide on debut

runners.name: Whet The Appetite
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding; dam 2m3f hurdle winner, half-sister to winners Gilgamboa (high-class 2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Stealthy Tom (useful 2m4f-3m1f chase); one of three home-bred debutants for yard; check market.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park gelding, dam 2m3f hurdle winner, market may prove best guide

runners.name: Wolfpack
runners.spotlight: Flat winner for James Ferguson last year; was running okay on hurdles debut when unseating at Leopardstown but tailed off twice since; tongue tie now tried.
runners.comment: Flat winner, unseated on hurdles debut, tailed off twice since, tongue tie now tried

runners.name: Thepoetslodge
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; pulled up at Gowran on debut and tailed off over this C&D 19 days ago; hard to make a case for him.
runners.comment: Pulled up on debut, tailed off over C&D latest, hard to make a case for him

runners.name: Georg Zhukov
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat performer didn't run that badly in two previous maiden hurdles at Ballinrobe and Killarney but he's likely to be seen to better effect when tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Modest Flat performer, not disgraced in two maiden hurdles, probably one for h'caps

runners.name: Good Vibrations
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and beaten 34l when midfield over C&D (yielding) on debut here 19 days ago; may need a bit more time and experience.
runners.comment: 22-1 and never better than midfield over C&D on debut, may need more time

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and tailed off on debut in a 4yo maiden hurdle at Punchestown; hard to fancy on that effort.
runners.comment: Huge odds and tailed off at Punchestown, hard to fancy on that effort

races.name: BAR 1 Betting Hurdle

runners.name: Absurde
runners.spotlight: Classy dual-purpose sort who has run creditably in the last three Melbourne Cups (rated 107 on the Flat); below best after a break in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham but should be sharper now and Harry Cobden has won on him before; top rated at 153 and obvious chance.
runners.comment: Classy dual-purpose sort, sharper for latest, Cobden has won on him before, obvious chance

runners.name: Ndaawi
runners.spotlight: Won the Galway Hurdle last year off 145 (awarded race by stewards) and has also won twice on the level (rated 91); trip and ground fine here and he finished well ahead of Absurde in the County Hurdle last month, though he had a fitness edge that day; talented claimer takes off 5lb and every chance at the weights.
runners.comment: Galway Hurdle winner, ahead of Absurde in County last time, every chance at weights

runners.name: Soir De Garde
runners.spotlight: Tramore maiden hurdle winner (16.5f, yielding) was no match for stablemate El Fabiolo at Leopardstown (18.5f, sft-hvy) last month; still relatively early days for this half-brother to State Man but is conceding 5lb to higher rated rivals here; Absurde looks yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Tramore maiden winner, well behind El Fabiolo latest, Absurde looks yard's main hope

runners.name: Busselton
runners.spotlight: Five-time chase winner's last two runs for Joseph O'Brien came in Cross Country races at Punchestown and Cheltenham; now with Enda Bolger and faces a tough task on these terms over a trip that looks too sharp.
runners.comment: Last two runs over the banks, new yard, trip a query, tough task at the weights

runners.name: Sageborough
runners.spotlight: Was impressive when winning on debut at Wexford but has come up short in graded company twice since; capable of better but rated 126 so a lot to find at these weights and third place probably the best he can hope for.
runners.comment: Impressive on debut, struggled in two graded races, rated 126, a lot to find

runners.name: No Looking Back
runners.spotlight: Smart form in his younger days, including a Grade 2 win, but has lost his way; soundly beaten at Leopardstown last time when well behind Soir De Garde and he's poorly treated by these race conditions.
runners.comment: Smart in younger days, has lost way, poorly treated by these race conditions

runners.name: Therellbguddaysyet
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner at Thurles in 2024 his sole success under Rules; lacks a recent run and rated just 108; would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, rated just 108, lacks a recent run, would be a shock winner

races.name: O'Flynn Motors Mallow Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Kimi De Mai
runners.spotlight: Won three bumpers when trained in France; struggled in first two starts over hurdles for this yard but decent third at Punchestown in January and best effort to date when an 8.5l third of 19 from well out of the handicap in a listed handicap hurdle at Leopardstown (2m, heavy); leading claims, though unproven on decent ground.
runners.comment: Fine run from out of the handicap at the DRF latest, big chance if handles better ground

runners.name: Poetic Twist
runners.spotlight: Identical RPRs (104) when fourth in a bumper on debut here in January and when beaten 12l into second in another bumper at Ayr, both on soft ground; bred to make a hurdler and in good hands; could go well.
runners.comment: Identical RPRs of 104 in bumpers here and at Ayr, bred to make a hurdler, could go well

runners.name: Somersby Roost
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; 0-5 over hurdles; rated 99 and was beaten 4.25l off that mark in a handicap hurdle at Navan 15 days ago; minor place chance back in maiden company.
runners.comment: Point winner, 0-5 over hurdles, beaten off 99 in h'cap latest, minor place chance

runners.name: Tountinna
runners.spotlight: Got to within 1.75l of Ballyfad in a Punchestown bumper (16.5, heavy) in November; below that level next two bumper runs but encouraging effort on hurdles debut when an 8.75l fourth behind three geldings at this venue (2m4f, yielding) last month; enters calculations now.
runners.comment: Bumper form with Ballyfad, decent hurdles debut here latest, enters calculations

runners.name: Courtmac Gesture
runners.spotlight: Promise in bumpers but didn't get her head in front; best effort yet over hurdles on latest when a 12l third of 8 at Leopardstown (2m5f, soft); place shout with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: Best effort yet over hurdles on latest at Leopardstown, place shout with repeat of that

runners.name: Lily Milan
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; signs of ability in bumpers and both maiden hurdles but needs to improve plenty on what she has achieved so far to win this.
runners.comment: Point winner, ability in both maiden hurdles and in bumpers, more needed to win this

runners.name: Crystal Gold
runners.spotlight: Placed in a point-to-point; definite ability in both completed maiden hurdles when third at Punchestown (19.5f, sft-hvy) and fourth at Clonmel (2m1f, heavy) on latest; in between those runs unseated at Limerick; place claims.
runners.comment: Promise in both completed starts over hurdles, place chance on those efforts

runners.name: Ardeen Joy
runners.spotlight: With a top yard but looks an unlikely winner based on her two runs at Kilbeggan and Ballinrobe last May; Poetic Twist looks stable's main hope.
runners.comment: Top yard but unlikely winner based on two previous runs, Poetic Twist yard's main hope

runners.name: Quiet In Bresil
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for all three starts and ran accordingly in a bumper at Limerick and in maiden hurdles at Punchestown and here; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Huge odds for all three starts and ran accordingly, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Toor Idaho
runners.spotlight: Rated 88 after 10 runs over hurdles; pulled up the last thrice and a leap of faith required to back her here.
runners.comment: Rated 88 after 10 runs over hurdles, pulled up the last thrice, leap of faith required

runners.name: Questionable
runners.spotlight: Flat winner (peak rating 74) was 100-1 and tailed off on hurdles debut at Wexford 19 days ago; hard to fancy on the back of that effort.
runners.comment: Flat winner, 100-1 and tailed off on hurdles debut 19 days ago, hard to fancy

runners.name: Chosen Princess
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and failed to land a blow on debut at Thurles in December; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 50-1 and no show on debut at Thurles in December, others appeal more

runners.name: Dictionary Corner
runners.spotlight: Refused to race at Limerick and pulled up at Thurles; new yard now; a risky proposition.
runners.comment: Refused to race at Limerick, pulled up at Thurles, new yard now, risky proposition

runners.name: Wings Of Africa
runners.spotlight: Huge odds when tailed off on debut at Thurles and again when pulled up at Punchestown; may need more time.
runners.comment: Huge odds when tailed off at Thurles and pulled up on latest, may need more time

races.name: BAR 1 Betting Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Coyote Spirit
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner showed improved form to run away with a 2m Navan maiden hurdle last time on heavy; raised 7lb and this is tougher but is going the right way.
runners.comment: Ran away with a 2m Navan maiden last time on heavy; raised 7lb and this is tougher

runners.name: Rockymountainbleu
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner has been mostly consistent in defeat in handicaps; has been slowly dropping in the weights and cheekpieces may help eke out more.
runners.comment: Maiden winner has been mostly consistent in defeat in h'caps; cheekpieces may help

runners.name: Open Secret
runners.spotlight: Was a close second in a Grade 3 novice at Thurles but not as good at Cheltenham in the Martin Pipe; however, has a shout if he can bounce back; considered.
runners.comment: Close 2nd in a Grade 3 novice but not as good at Cheltenham; can bounce back; considered

runners.name: Its Bilbo
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed handicap at Limerick over 2m on testing ground buty was poor behind a stablemate over 2m4f at Cheltenham; has to rebound.
runners.comment: Won a Listed handicap at Limerick over 2m; poor over 2m4f at Cheltenham; has to rebound

runners.name: Im Slippy
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m maiden hurdle here and was second in a Grade 3 at Fairyhouse since; however, he has to rebound from a poor handicap run last time.
runners.comment: Won a 2m maiden here and 2nd in a Grade 3 since; has to rebound from a poor h'cap run

runners.name: Sequoiaspirit
runners.spotlight: Is just 1-27 over hurdles and was well beaten here last time after a break; can improve on that but plenty others preferred.
runners.comment: Just 1-27 over hurdles and was well beaten here last time so others preferred

runners.name: D Art D Art
runners.spotlight: Was in great form last season, running a cracker in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; however, he has been poor in two runs this term and could find this trip sharp.
runners.comment: In great form last season but poor in two runs this term and could find this trip sharp

runners.name: Autumn Twist
runners.spotlight: Posted a decent run in the Lartigue at Listowel on his last hurdle start; had a pipe-opener on the Flat at Dundalk lately; can be involved.
runners.comment: Decent run in the Lartigue at Listowel last h'dle start; had a pipe-opener on the Flat

runners.name: Cappa Hill
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps here and at Clonmel over shorter last autumn before a poor run on testing ground at Punchestown; has a squeak if ready after break.
runners.comment: Won handicaps here and at Clonmel over shorter last autumn; a squeak if ready after break

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner last year, from 2m-2m4f; posted a decent Flat run last time at the Curragh; is ground-versatile and could run well on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner; decent Flat run last time; could run well on handicap bow

runners.name: Ifallgoeswell
runners.spotlight: Has been solid in defeat in three maiden hurdles lately from 2m-2m4f; could go well on handicap bow; watch the market.
runners.comment: Solid in defeat in three maidens lately; could go well on h'cap bow; watch the market

runners.name: County Final
runners.spotlight: Was runner-up in a Grade 2 novice over 2m7f at Limerick but did win his maiden over this trip at Navan; had an excuse (coughing) last time; hood added; considered.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a Grade 2 over 2m7f but has won at this trip; an excuse last time; considered

runners.name: Wholelotofbusiness
runners.spotlight: Won a Killarney novice hurdle and second (beaten 7.5l) in a Down Royal handicap, both over 2m1f last autumn; has a chance if ready after a break.
runners.comment: Won a Killarney novice and 2nd in a Down Royal handicap; a chance if ready after break

runners.name: Elusive Ogie
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner ran well in three handicaps last autumn from 2m-2m4f; has to find more on his return in this tough race.
runners.comment: Maiden winner ran well in three handicaps last autumn; has to find more on return

runners.name: Larzac
runners.spotlight: Promising runner-up on sole French start; pitched into Grade 1s first two starts for this yard; was disappointing in a Naas maiden last time but is capable of a lot better; jockey booking takes the eye so not ruled out; check the market.
runners.comment: Disappointing in a Naas maiden last time but jockey booking takes the eye so not ruled out

runners.name: Monbeg Park
runners.spotlight: Better known as a chaser but has shown smart hurdles form in the past; would have a squeak if back in top form off a lower hurdle mark.
runners.comment: Better known as a chaser but would have a squeak if back to form off a lower hurdle mark

runners.name: Sainte Lucie
runners.spotlight: Had been below her best in first two starts this term but came back to life when not getting the best of runs in the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; same rider aboard; has to be on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Didn't get the best of runs in the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; has to be on the shortlist

runners.name: Butter Fingers
runners.spotlight: Has been mostly over fences without success this season; posted a solid run back to hurdles over this trip at Naas last time; needs more in a stronger race.
runners.comment: Solid run back to hurdles over this trip at Naas last time; needs more in a stronger race

runners.name: Pray Tell
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 3 novice hurdle (3m) here in December 2024; good chase run two starts back before falling last time; might find this trip too sharp.
runners.comment: Grade 3 hurdle (3m) winner here; good chase run two starts back; might find this too sharp

races.name: Hibernian Hotel Mallow Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jeriko De Baune
runners.spotlight: Won at Clonmel in November off 3lb lower; bounced back to form after three poor runs with a 5l third of 10 over C&D 17 days ago (1.5l behind Toor Khov); not guaranteed to back that effort up but place claims at least if he does.
runners.comment: Won off 3lb lower in Nov', back to form here off this mark latest, go well if repeats that
runners.owner: Crohane Town & Country Syndicate/Mrs E A Lawlor

runners.name: Toor Khov
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner; landed a beginners' chase at Thurles (2m5f, heavy) in January; had some of these behind when good second back over hurdles over this C&D latest (Jeriko De Baune third); quicker ground fine and in the mix again off just 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, a few of these behind over C&D latest, up 1lb, in the mix again

runners.name: Glenmalure Lady
runners.spotlight: Three-time point-to-point winner ran respectably in two of her three maiden hurdles but 33-1 and pulled up on handicap hurdle debut at Fairyhouse (3m2f, yielding) on New Year's Day; off since and 2lb drop not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut at Fairyhouse in Jan', 2lb drop not enough to tempt

runners.name: Sarandon
runners.spotlight: Glimmers of ability in maidens but yet to really fire since tackling handicaps; pulled up and beaten 22l on last two starts; step up to 3m needs to bring about improvement (dam half-sister to 3m2f winner).
runners.comment: Mild promise in maidens, yet to fire in handicaps, needs to improve for step up to 3m

runners.name: Themanintheanorak
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner landed a handicap hurdle at Fairyhouse in February (2m4f, heavy); raised 7lb and has struggled in two starts since, including when 6.5l behind Onlymammycanloveme here (2m4f) on latest; moves up in trip now; more needed off this mark.
runners.comment: Struggled twice off revised mark since Fairyhouse win, more needed up in trip

runners.name: West Cork Wildway
runners.spotlight: Veteran gained a third win over hurdles over C&D (yielding-soft) in November off 7lb lower, his first win for over four years; went close off 3lb higher chase mark at Limerick 24 days ago; not one to dismiss on ground he'll handle.
runners.comment: Veteran, C&D win in November off 6lb lower, good chase run latest, can't dismiss

runners.name: Ishan
runners.spotlight: Won three times in 2023 on soft and heavy ground but has come up short since; better effort on latest when a 3.5l third of 16 in a handicap chase at Limerick (2m7f, heavy); 2lb below last win but probably needs rain.
runners.comment: Three wins in 2023 on soft-heavy, back to form over fences latest, probably needs rain

runners.name: Ossifer Hops
runners.spotlight: Won a Downpatrick maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2023 but on a barren run since; was on the retreat when carried out at Clonmel latest; pulled up on previous start; not easy to fancy on recent showings and 5lb wrong.
runners.comment: On long losing run, beaten when carried out latest, 5lb wrong here, hard to fancy

runners.name: Onlymammycanloveme
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point and maiden hurdle winner notched up a first handicap win over 2m4f (yielding) here 17 days ago; raised 7lb and moves up in trip but should be competitive again, though wouldn't want the track to dry out (non-runner on account of gd-yld before).
runners.comment: Won over 2m4f here latest, up 7lb and in trip, competitive again but drying ground a query

runners.name: One And Only
runners.spotlight: Course winner (2m4f, soft) in December 2024; got back to winning ways at Limerick (2m7f, heavy) last month; up 6lb and this is a deeper race but conditions fine so can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Course winner, scored off 6lb lower at Limerick latest, this harder but can't discount

runners.name: West Clare
runners.spotlight: Sole success at Listowel (2m6f, soft) in September 2024 off 7lb lower; only run four times since, all handicap chases, and off the track since a 31l defeat at Killarney in July 2025; has ability but might need this run.
runners.comment: Won off 7lb lower in Sept' 2023, off since heavy chase defeat last July, may need this

runners.name: Committee Meeting
runners.spotlight: Ran well in a maiden hurdle at Thurles in November 2024 but has struggled since, though has been set some tough tasks; this more realistic and worth a market check on return from a break on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed in a maiden hurdle, tough tasks since, this more realistic, check the market

runners.name: Whatsavailable
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner, all three victories coming at Fairyhouse; out of form in 2025 and his Gowran fifth on latest was a remote one; now 4lb below last win but needs to rediscover best.
runners.comment: Three-time Fairyhouse winner, lost form in 2025, remote fifth latest, needs to find best

runners.name: Getaway Charlie
runners.spotlight: No win since October 2023; pulled up on final start of last season over C&D (soft) and similar outcome on seasonal reappearance at Fairyhouse in November; off since and best watched for now.
runners.comment: No win since Oct' 2023, pulled up the last twice, lacks a recent run, best watched

runners.name: Thegraveyardbookie
runners.spotlight: No joy since 2m3f Limerick maiden hurdle win on heavy in December 2023; off since falling in a chase at Killarney in May 2025; likely best watched.
runners.comment: On long losing run, fell in a chase when last seen in May 2025, likely best watched

runners.name: West Is Awake
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner was well beaten in this last year but ran a nice race on sole start since at Fairyhouse (3m2f, yielding) in January; 3lb rise for that 6l second seems harsh and this is harder but he has to be considered for yard that won this in 2024.
runners.comment: Up 3lb for 6l Fairyhouse second on latest, this is harder but has to be considered

races.name: BAR 1 Betting Chase (Grade 3)

runners.name: Classic Getaway
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 chase winner was poor at Tramore on New Year's Day but was much better when third to a stablemate at Gowran last time; has to give away plenty of weight but is still the one to beat with a top rider aboard; hasn't won over this trip but looks likely to stay.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner has to give away plenty of weight but is still the one to beat

runners.name: Nowwhatdoyouthink
runners.spotlight: Point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner got off the mark over fences at this track (2m5f) in November; better effort when second in a Thurles novice last time over a similar trip; can race keenly so not sure will stay 3m but is the most likely to give the top-weight a race.
runners.comment: Can race keenly so not sure will stay 3m but the most likely to give the top-weight a race

runners.name: Broomfield Bijou
runners.spotlight: Was a decent second in a Thurles handicap in February but was well beaten at Leopardstown since and has loads to find against the top two in this race; due to run in the 2.40 at Fairyhouse Saturday.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Leopardstown last time; has loads to find against the top two in this race

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase

runners.name: Wingmen
runners.spotlight: Has won a point, two bumpers and a maiden hurdle plus placed in two Grade 1 novice hurdles last year; solid in defeat in his first three chase runs but below that form in a classy Cheltenham handicap last time; looks the better of the Elliott pair and a big player even down in trip.
runners.comment: Solid in his first three chase runs; not as good at Cheltenham last time; big player

runners.name: Funiculi Funicula
runners.spotlight: Classy hurdler won a Clonmel 2m1f maiden before a close third in an Aintree Grade 1 novice over 2m4f last season; was an unlucky loser on second chase start at Gowran last time behind a stablemate; down in trip and a big player.
runners.comment: Unlucky loser on second chase start at Gowran last time; down in trip and a big player

runners.name: Mister Pink
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and a 2m4f Punchestown maiden hurdle when with Willie Mullins; good run in defeat in a Thurles novice when last seen; makes chasing debut for a new yard after an absence; has a bit to find.
runners.comment: Useful sort makes chasing debut for a new yard after an absence; has a bit to find

runners.name: Scalpnagoon
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner but lost his form badly in that sphere last few runs; was poor on chasing debut at Thurles in February; not easy to fancy and a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Poor on chasing debut at Thurles in February; not easy to fancy and a tongue-tie added

runners.name: Ocastle Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner in France; yet to score for this yard but some good runs in defeat including in the first of two chase starts last season; yard has a better chance with Funiculi Funcicula.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat including on chase debut last term; yard second-string

runners.name: Major Wells
runners.spotlight: A hint of ability in bumpers; well beaten in two hurdles; unseated on chasing debut; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Hint of ability in bumpers; poor in 2 hurdles; unseated on chasing debut; look elsewhere

runners.name: The Burrow
runners.spotlight: Point winner showed just a hint of ability in three maiden hurdles and a chase; can only be watched back from an absence.
runners.comment: Hint of ability in 3 maiden hurdles and a chase; can only be watched back from an absence

races.name: Ampion Ltd. Electrical And Testing (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Lemmy Caution
runners.spotlight: Point winner last year was sold for 250,000GBP afterwards; decent efforts in three bumpers in varying ground conditions but hasn't progressed in that time; needs to do more.
runners.comment: Decent efforts in three bumpers but hasn't progressed in that time; needs to do more

runners.name: Too Hot To Trot
runners.spotlight: Just denied in a Ballindenisk point on debut in November and moved to this top yard since; the jockey who rode him on that occasion stays aboard; one to consider, especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Just denied in a point in November; one to consider, especially if strong in the market

runners.name: Ellis Redding
runners.spotlight: Comfortably beat a runner from this yard (handicap hurdle winner since off 89) to take a Punchestown point in February; comes from a decent damline and one to consider on bumper debut.
runners.comment: Point winner comes from a decent damline and one to consider on bumper debut

runners.name: Gonewild
runners.spotlight: Started at 100/1 but ran with credit in a high quality Leopardstown maiden hurdle on debut at Christmas; with improvement to come he should be more competitive in this sphere; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Solid run in a high quality Leopardstown maiden hurdle on debut; can be more competitive

runners.name: Ludus Magnus
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts but still soundly beaten in bumpers at Punchestown and Naas; this is less competitive but still has to do more.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in bumpers at Punchestown and Naas; this is less competitive but needs more

runners.name: Harrys Diamond
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; sister to useful 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner Built By Ballymore, half-sister to winners Dancing On My Own (smart 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase) and You Raised Me Up (useful bumper and 2m/2m3f hurdle); dam useful bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner; well-bred sort from the champion trainer so greatly respected on debut.
runners.comment: Well-bred sort from the champion trainer so greatly respected on debut

runners.name: Oh The Darsi Boy
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam point winner; sire is capable of getting useful winners; hood on for debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Sire is capable of getting useful winners; hood on for debut; best watched

runners.name: Ben Sawborne
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam French 2m3f Listed hurdle winner, half-sister to winners Gaelic Ocean (useful French 2m1f-2m2f hurdle/chase) and Great Jane (2m1f/2m5f chase; dam of high-class French jumper Galop Marin); cost E22,000 as a 3yo; worth a check in the market for a local yard.
runners.comment: Cost E22,000 as a 3yo; worth a check in the market for a local yard

runners.name: Airds Moss
runners.spotlight: One of two runners for yard; showed improved form on his second point start in October when a close third at Umma House; half-brother to a couple of decent jumps winners; could run well.
runners.comment: Close third on last point start; related to a pair of decent jumps winners; could run well

runners.name: Tazman Dan
runners.spotlight: Improved nicely from bumper debut to be beaten only 11l at Wexford last time; needs to take another decent step forward to win but likely to be involved.
runners.comment: Beaten only 11l at Wexford last time; needs another step forward but likely to be involved

runners.name: Barnahash Prince
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Barnahash Primrose; dam bumper/2m6f Grade 3 hurdle winner, half-sister to bumper/hurdle winners Brackenmoss (2m) and Ask The Gatherer (2m3f-2m5f); useful pedigree and worth a market look on debut for a local yard.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree and worth a market look on debut for a local yard

runners.name: Bobs House
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off on Thurles bumper debut at 150/1; not a difficult decision to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Tailed-off on Thurles bumper debut at 150/1; look elsewhere

runners.name: Cloghoula More
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Strike Back (bumper and 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase) and Beef To The Heels (2m hurdle); not a bad pedigree and could run respectably on debut.
runners.comment: Not a bad pedigree and could run respectably on debut

runners.name: Line Speed
runners.spotlight: The best of three bumper runs was when beaten 13l at Limerick on second start; questions after being tailed-off at Gowran since; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l at Limerick but questions after being tailed-off at Gowran since; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Long Goodbye
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on Thurles debut but came up against a quality rival (since fourth in Champion Bumper) when beaten 18l at Down Royal; there is unlikely to be a rival of that calibre in this field so should get closer.
runners.comment: Came up against a quality rival when beaten 18l at Down Royal; should get closer here

runners.name: Tiger Twins
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on sole bumper start at Listowel last year but better efforts in two maiden hurdles since; has to continue that progress back to this sphere.
runners.comment: Improved runs in two maiden hurdles; has to continue that progress back to this sphere

runners.name: Lucky Vick
runners.spotlight: Wore a hood but raced keenly when beaten 32l on debut at Thurles; the headgear is retained but has to improve plenty.
runners.comment: Beaten 32l on debut at Thurles; the headgear is retained but has to improve plenty

races.name: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Handicap

runners.name: Luna Beaux
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish filly who has won two of her four starts for her new stable, most recently getting up close home at Wolverhampton 19 days ago; only 1lb higher and makes more appeal than many.
runners.comment: 2-4 for new yard; only a 1lb rise since latest AW win; more appealing than many

runners.name: Solar Invincible
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a 5f win at Chelmsford in December; two solid efforts this year, more recently when fourth at Lingfield nine days ago (tight for room close home); promise on turf last year; still open to further progress.
runners.comment: 5f AW win in December; two solid efforts this year; still open to further progress

runners.name: Rogue Rebellion
runners.spotlight: Two good runs since dropping back to 5f, winning at Lingfield in January and second at Newcastle seven weeks ago; sold out of James Owen's stable for 10,000gns soon after that latest run; more needed but not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Two good runs for James Owen this year; sold 10,000gns since latest; up in class today

runners.name: Runamara
runners.spotlight: His 2yo win came at this track (5.7f, soft); running himself back into form for new stable, never out of the first two when second to an unexposed opponent at Kempton on Wednesday (6f); still inclined to over-race but dropping back in trip might help.
runners.comment: Course winner (5.7f, soft); good second at Kempton (6f) on Wednesday; drops in trip

runners.name: Dragon Spin
runners.spotlight: Beaten under 3l in each of her three nursery runs last year (AW and soft); needs to have improved over the winter to make a winning return to action.
runners.comment: Ran with credit in three nurseries last year; needs to have improved over the winter

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Three nursery wins for William Knight last year and second to a handicap blot at Lingfield when last seen ten weeks ago; sold for 12,000gns in February; best turf form on good or slower.
runners.comment: Three wins for W Knight last year; sold 12,000gns since latest start; others appeal more

runners.name: Bellini Spirit
runners.spotlight: Promise on her second start for Anthony Carson, finishing second of seven at Chelmsford last October (6f, AW); easy to back and finished well beaten in a 1m maiden at Lingfield on stable debut six weeks ago; needs a market check dropping in trip for her handicap/turf debut.
runners.comment: Best run over 6f; unexposed; needs close market check down in trip on handicap/turf debut

runners.name: Believeinmenow
runners.spotlight: Attracted support on his 2yo debut and ran well for second of ten in a 5f maiden at Thirsk (good ground); no progress afterwards but his one run in a handicap saw him keep on well for a closing ninth, beaten 3l, in a Class 2 at York's Ebor meeting; gelded over the winter; stiff 5f should be fine and he's likely to be held up which won't be a bad thing if the forecast strong headwind arrives; stable 6-43 at Bath with another 19 finishing either second or third; interesting dropped into a Class 5.
runners.comment: Bits of 2yo form make him of major interest; gelded since last run; should go well

runners.name: Jenny Jerome
runners.spotlight: Five runs in fillies' novice events as a 2yo (5f-6f), showing her best form when a close third at Nottingham in July (third start; 5f,  good to soft); more realistic chance now handicapping; betting can guide after her six months off.
runners.comment: Some promise in 5 runs in fillies' novice events in 2025; market to guide now handicapping
runners.owner: Pcf Racing Ltd Ceramic Tile Merchants

races.name: Netbet Easter Festival Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Wait Geordie
runners.spotlight: March foal; Â£50,000 yearling; first foal of a 5f 2yo winner (RPR 95), herself a sister to Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint winner Mischief Magic; stable 3-10 (and two thirds) with 2yos at Bath; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Â£50,000 yearling; dam a well-related and useful 5f 2yo winner; yard 3-10 with 2yos here

runners.name: Paper View
runners.spotlight: April foal; 60,000euros yearling; thirteenth foal; half-brother to seven winners including Balty Boys (6f-1m1f including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 117), Artistic Rifles (7f-1m1f including 2yo/Group 3; 113) and Shore Step (5.7f/6f; 99); dam 6f 2yo winner (76), sister to 6f Group 3 winner Time Prisoner; not obviously a precocious type on paper but trainer won the Brocklesby and this colt must have been showing plenty at home to be starting out so soon.
runners.comment: 60,000euros half-brother to seven winners, two at Group 3 level; stable won the Brocklesby

runners.name: Devon Angel
runners.spotlight: January foal; Â£16,000 yearling; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 83), half-sister to useful winners Lewisham (5f/6f) and Crack On Crack On (7f/1m); stable may have stronger claims with Bayside.
runners.comment: Â£16,000 yearling; dam a 5f 2yo winner; stable may have stronger claims with Bayside

runners.name: Magical Life
runners.spotlight: March foal; third foal; half-sister to 1m2f winner Life Is Rosie (RPR 75); dam unraced half-sister to Japanese 1m1f winner Prepared; winning 2yo newcomers from the stable a rarity these days.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a minor 1m2f winner; others have more persuasive paper claims

runners.name: Bayside
runners.spotlight: January foal; 11,000gns yearling; second foal of a maiden half-sister to winners Whazzis (1m Group 3) and Whazzat (7f 2yo Listed; dam of 6f 2yo Group 2 winner James Garfield); one of two newcomers for Rod Millman.
runners.comment: 11,000gns yearling; dam a well-related maiden; one of two runners for Rod Millman

runners.name: Mystical Magical
runners.spotlight: April foal; Â£26,000 yearling; second foal of a maiden half-sister to US 1m Grade 2 winner Up In Time; trainer, who also runs Postcard, sent out Bill The Bull to finish fourth in the Brocklesby.
runners.comment: Â£26,000 yearling; dam a half-sister to a US Grade 2 winner; stable also run Postcard

runners.name: Dei Gratia Regina
