runners.spotlight: January foal; 42,000gns yearling; second foal of a 7f-1m winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 99), herself closely related to smart 6f/7f winner Laddies Poker Two (dam of 1,000 Guineas winner Winter); betting instructive.
runners.comment: 42,000gns yearling; dam a useful 7f-1m winner (RPR 99); betting to guide

runners.name: Postcard
runners.spotlight: May foal; 1,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to 1m 2yo winner Storm Alice (RPR 60); dam unplaced 5f-7f (47), half-sister to Abbaye winner Gilt Edge Girl; one of two runners for Adam Kirby.
runners.comment: 1,000gns yearling; half-sister to minor 1m 2yo winner Storm Alice; yard runs two here

runners.name: Dowman
runners.spotlight: February foal; 7,500gns yearling; first foal of a 5f winner (RPR 79), herself a half-sister to a Group 3 winner; likely type on paper; stable 3-26 with their 2yo runners at Bath.
runners.comment: 7,500gns yearling; first foal of a 5f winner (RPR 79); yard 3-26 with 2yos at Bath

runners.name: Innichen
runners.spotlight: February foal; 40,000gns foal, Â£65,000 yearling; first foal of a French Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 92), herself a half-sister to useful 7f/1m winner Shinning Ocean; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Â£65,000 yearling; dam a Listed-placed 2yo winner in France; betting to guide

races.name: Whitsbury Manor Stud / British EBF Lansdown Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Miss Attitude
runners.spotlight: Made good progress last summer, notably when beating male opposition in a 5f Listed event at Deauville in August; shaped nicely in a good 5f handicap at Southwell on her return to action two weeks ago; strong claims.
runners.comment: Beat males in Deauville Listed race last August; promising return last month; good claims

runners.name: Beautiful Diamond
runners.spotlight: Her three Listed wins have all come at Ayr, but she is fully effective elsewhere and ran right up to her best when a neck second at Ascot when last seen in October (5f, soft); can go well fresh and she is a leading contender despite her 3lb penalty.
runners.comment: Three-time Listed winner; ended 2025 in top form; penalty to defy but still a big player

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins (good to soft and soft) as a 2yo; better than ever when winning a Class 4 handicap at Wolverhampton (5f) 19 days ago; much more required in today's field.
runners.comment: The winner of three of her eight starts; upped in class and needs a big step forward

runners.name: Cindy Lou Who
runners.spotlight: Three of her four wins last year came at Bath, including over this trip; big step up in class to deal with on this first run since October.
runners.comment: Three course wins in a progressive 2025 campaign; big step up in class on her return

runners.name: Arugam Bay
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a small-field Listed event at Tipperary last July (5f, good; unpenalised); two defeats afterwards came over 6f in Group 3 company; dropping back to 5f won't be a problem; not discounted.
runners.comment: Made all in Listed race at Tipperary last July; absent since August; return to 5f a plus

runners.name: Hold A Dream
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo; made a fine return to action last May when second of 12 in a Listed event at Chelmsford (6f); placed at this level on a further three occasions, including when last seen in October; should give another good account for a stable that won this in 2010, 2013 and 2017.
runners.comment: Failed to win in 2025 but placed four times in Listed company; should give a good account

runners.name: Angel Shared
runners.spotlight: Tough front-running handicapper but nothing in her form to think she was the answer to today's puzzle.
runners.comment: Tough front-running handicapper but she looks up against it in this field

runners.name: Shine On Me
runners.spotlight: Highly tried as a 2yo, placed in Listed/Group 3 company before taking advantage of a straightforward opportunity at Wolverhampton 160 days ago (unimpressive at odds of 1-10); cheekpieces return for her seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Placed in Listed/Group 3 company as a 2yo; cheekpieces return on seasonal debut

runners.name: Flowerhead
runners.spotlight: Won a Chepstow maiden on her second start; big step forward when second of 23 in the Group 2 Queen Mary at Royal Ascot 11 days later; no progress afterwards but today's return to 5f should be in her favour.
runners.comment: Second in Queen Mary last summer; no progress afterwards; return to 5f looks a plus

runners.name: Arduis Invicta
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins last year; seemed to excel herself in Listed company at Ayr in September and Redcar one month later; even those efforts need leaving well behind if she is to take this.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins in 2025 but even best efforts lag some way behind the standard required today

runners.name: Azure Angel
runners.spotlight: Back to something approaching her best when winning a 14-runner handicap at Southwell last September (blinkered first time); may have needed the run back at Southwell last month (first run for 164 days); she'll do well to win but a minor placing isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: Good handicap form when winning at Southwell in September; sharper for last month's return

runners.name: Palmeira
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in her ten 2yo starts but she was often highly tried, running her best race when second of 11 in a Listed event at Newbury (5f, good); looks to face another stiff task on her reappearance.
runners.comment: 0-10 as a 2yo; second in Newbury Listed event last summer; stiff task on her reappearance

runners.name: Billie Be Quick
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who advanced her form with her sixth to Beautiful Diamond in an Ayr Listed event last September (blinkered first time); over 4l to find with that rival though and she looks up against it; cheekpieces the headgear of choice today.
runners.comment: Recorded peak RPR in Ayr Listed event where she finished over 4l behind Beautiful Diamond

runners.name: Rubys Profit
runners.spotlight: Speedy filly who won twice last year and regularly acquitted herself with credit in good handicaps; could yet make up into a black type performer but this won't be an easy race in which to try and dominate.
runners.comment: Did well in 5f handicaps last year; can progress again this year; other pace on show today

races.name: Floyds Turfcare And Weed Solutions Handicap

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: Acquitted himself with credit in the face of a tough task at Lingfield last month; back down in class today and 4lb lower than for his latest win (October); one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran well in the face of a stiff task at Lingfield last time; dangerous mark; considered

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: Course winner (5.7f) off this mark last August; low-key return to action at Wolverhampton last month; the booking of Billy Loughnane points to a better showing today.
runners.comment: Course win off this mark last August; sharper for recent return and Billy Loughnane booked

runners.name: Twilight Madness
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; two wins in December and in the money on his last three starts; just 1-15 on turf as opposed to 8-37 on AW; others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Arrives in good form from AW but he has a much better strike-rate on AW than turf
runners.owner: P R Hedger & P C F Racing Ltd

runners.name: Dragon God
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; two runs for Tony Carroll last month saw him finish unplaced but the first of them wasn't without hope; down in weights and appeals as the type to come good at some point soon; drops to 5f for the first time; market support would look significant.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden; not been with Tony Carroll long and remains capable of better; new trip

runners.name: Diamondsinthesand
runners.spotlight: Won three times in the second half of 2025, including at this track (5.7f); looks high enough in the weights on his return from a five-month absence.
runners.comment: Three wins in the second half of 2025; high in weights ahead of his seasonal return

runners.name: Dandy Dinmont
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to win a Thirsk handicap (6f, good to firm) last August off a 1lb higher mark; mixed bag since but dropping back into Class 6 company will be in his favour.
runners.comment: On a good mark and he should appreciate drop back into Class 6 company; solid claims

runners.name: Reporter
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his 18 starts but that came off a 2lb higher mark last summer; raced freely on his return to action 16 days ago and dropped away in the straight; likely to last longer today.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but on a handy mark and recent return should have brought him on

runners.name: The Feminine Urge
runners.spotlight: Won three times for George Scott, including two 5f handicaps off similar marks last year; sold for 2,000gns in October; absent for 208 days but Rossa Ryan is booked and his record for the stable (15-102 for a level-stake profit of 48.25pts) offers hope she will be ready to roll.
runners.comment: On a handy mark; makes stable debut after 208 days off; Rossa Ryan a good booking for yard

runners.name: Symbol Of Hope
runners.spotlight: He has seven course wins to his name but 2025 was disappointing and he's rather been going through the motions on AW this year; not impossible the return to Bath will pique his interest.
runners.comment: Seven course wins; not as good as he was but the return to Bath could perk him up a bit

runners.name: Tilsworth Ony Ta
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, including at this track, but the last was back in July 2024; quiet on AW this winter.
runners.comment: Not at his best this winter and he looks up against it once again

runners.name: Call Time
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft ground, including C&D; effective on a faster surface too but fully exposed and may come on for this first run since November.
runners.comment: C&D winner but he is fully exposed and may come on for this seasonal return
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Hidden Verse
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, including here (5.7f, firm); 6lb higher than for the second of them and he will need to be at his best on his return from eight months off.
runners.comment: Course winner; 6lb higher than for his last win and returning from eight months off

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; two 5f wins on turf last summer; on his last winning mark but he was disappointing when last seen and his record fresh suggests this may not be his day.
runners.comment: C&D winner; on a winning mark but absent 132 days and this may not be his day

races.name: Droneart Show May 8th & 9th Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Colourband
runners.spotlight: Third of 11 in a novice at Salisbury (1m, good) in October on second start; gelded before he improved again when second of ten to a good prospect at Kempton (1m, AW) in December, which gives him the best form in this line-up; major claims, although 1m2f is not absolutely certain to suit and he's tended to hang right on his last two starts.
runners.comment: Best form in this; 1m2f is not absolutely certain to suit and he's tended to hang right

runners.name: Baltic Fleet
runners.spotlight: 75,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; promise at Windsor (1m, soft) before his always-prominent, short-head win in a Yarmouth maiden (1m1f, good to soft) later in September; should stay; one of the two who dominate calculations.
runners.comment: Short-head win in a Yarmouth maiden (1m1f, good to soft) in September latest; should stay

runners.name: Chilli King
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Golden Horn half-brother to winners Hariboux (7f-1m1f including 2yo/AW/US stakes; RPR 97), Chilli Queen (1m4f; 92) and Tara Iti (1m 2yo Flat, 66; 2m/2m1f hurdle); dam 1m2f turf and 1m4f AW winner (88), sister to 1m4f/2m winner; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Pretty useful pedigree; Golden Horn half-brother to winners; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Baron Wagstaff
runners.spotlight: Has a pretty useful pedigree and showed some ability at Kempton (1m, AW; 200-1) early last month, but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Some ability at Kempton (1m, AW; 200-1) in March but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Master Jorrocks
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Cityscape half-brother to 5f winner Therehegoes (RPR 64); dam fourth at 5f on AW (47), closely related to 1m winner Sheriff, out of 6f-winning half-sister to 6f Listed winner Mugharreb; 7-1, tailed off in a 2m point-to-point bumper five week ago; best watched.
runners.comment: Flat bred; 7-1, tailed off in a 2m point-to-point bumper five week ago

runners.name: Cape Fear
runners.spotlight: Closely related to bumper winner Topkapi, half-brother to 11.4f winner City Spirit (RPR 70); dam placed at 1m-1m6f (61); 16-1, slow-starting fifth of seven in novice at Brighton (1m, good to firm) in October, which he will surely improve on but two of today's rivals have shown much more.
runners.comment: Brighton fifth as 2yo; will surely improve but two of today's rivals have shown much more

runners.name: Summer Evening
runners.spotlight: Looks well established as modest maiden at best, albeit mostly on AW.
runners.comment: Looks well established as modest maiden at best, albeit mostly on AW

runners.name: Wonderful Wendy
runners.spotlight: 125-1, tailed off in Southwell maiden (1m3f, AW) last August.
runners.comment: 125-1, tailed off in Southwell maiden (1m3f, AW) last August

races.name: R.J. King & Sons Handicap

runners.name: Law Supreme
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; 3-7 since cheekpieces went on, with all those wins over 9.4f on the Wolverhampton Tapeta; good second, however, over 1m at Newcastle (AW) nine days ago; major player if that form is transferred to turf and he was fully effective (C&D winner) on turf in 2024.
runners.comment: Major player on recent AW form and he was fully effective (C&D winner) on turf in 2024

runners.name: The Dark Baron
runners.spotlight: Most consistent since switched to the AW in October (1332321; most under Laura Coughlan) with the wins at Wolverhampton over 9.4f and 7f; creeping up the weights and although he had a 1m2f turf win last September, there is something for him to prove in this discipline.
runners.comment: 1332321 since switched to the AW in October; something to prove back on turf

runners.name: The Hare Rail
runners.spotlight: Yet to win after 14 starts but has managed four seconds and is often thereabouts; place hopes with a recent run under his belt.
runners.comment: Yet to win after 14 starts but has managed four seconds and is often thereabouts
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

runners.name: Tribal Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Returned from seven months off to win from off the pace at Epsom (8.5f, good) last April; now bids to do much the same, after four lesser shows last season, and he's back to that last winning mark.
runners.comment: Seven months off before Epsom win off this mark last April; attempts to repeat the trick

runners.name: Little Miss India
runners.spotlight: 3-9 since a hood went on, including wins under Ashley Lewis at Wolverhampton (1m4f and 9.4f) on two of her last three starts; unraced over a trip this short since her pre-handicap days and the hood is removed; also unraced on turf since last July and none of her turf form matches this mark.
runners.comment: 1m4f and 9.4f wins at Wolverhampton on two of her last three starts; more to prove on turf

runners.name: In The Post
runners.spotlight: 1m2f seemed to stretch him when he was seventh of 13 here on handicap debut last September; peak form was at 1m the time before; overall improvement is needed but he's had only four races, so could still have potential.
runners.comment: 1m2f seemed to stretch him on handicap debut last September; needs overall improvement

runners.name: Risen Again
runners.spotlight: Sole win (21 races) was over 1m2f at Yarmouth last May but subsequent efforts include two seconds; left Alice Haynes for 17,000gns in October and came last of 12 (hood removed) when upped to 1m5f for new yard in February; now has a bit to prove over a trip this short.
runners.comment: Last when upped to 1m5f for new yard in February; also bit to prove over a trip this short

runners.name: Look Back Smiling
runners.spotlight: Usually slowly away; lost his form last autumn but he's thrown in judged on plenty of earlier 2025 form, never mind peak efforts; first run since he left Gemma Tutty for 9,000gns in December and the market may guide.
runners.comment: Lost form last autumn but thrown in on plenty of earlier form; left G Tutty for 9,000gns
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mr John Hobbs

runners.name: Darvel
runners.spotlight: Two of his eight wins have been over C&D and they were first time out in April 2024 and April 2025; two wins last summer ensure that he remains much higher in the weights but that reappearance record here is striking.
runners.comment: C&D for two of his eight wins and they were first time out in April 2024 and April 2025

runners.name: Dark Rosa
runners.spotlight: Shot back up the weights after an easy win at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in November; had comparable form over 6f on turf last season and finished on heels of the leaders in 1m2f AW race in January on latest start, but others may well be a bit better handicapped.
runners.comment: Close up in January on latest start but others may well be a bit better handicapped

runners.name: Crimson Road
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner over 1m2f on good to firm in 2024 but no impact in 2025; didn't really take to hurdling and left Susan Corbett for Â£5,000 in December; hood and tongue-tie are removed on this debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Left Susan Corbett for Â£5,000 in December; hood and tongue-tie are removed

races.name: Ella Bury Always In Our Hearts Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Purple Sky
runners.spotlight: String of poor efforts since an Irish win over further last summer, finishing well behind Volendam on the AW last time; first-time headgear will need to make a big difference on this return to turf.
runners.comment: String of poor efforts since an Irish win over further last summer; headgear goes on

runners.name: Volendam
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's been turned over as favourite a couple of times on the AW of late, latterly looking to have no complaints at a very short price; claims again back on turf for the first time for her current yard but there can only be so many excuses.
runners.comment: Claims back on turf for the first time for this yard but there can only be so many excuses

runners.name: Happy Chandler
runners.spotlight: Maiden who had plenty of chances at a similar level last year; well held first time out in his three previous seasons and he is some way down the pecking order back from his latest break.
runners.comment: Maiden who had plenty of chances at this level last year; some way down the pecking order

runners.name: American Rose
runners.spotlight: Serial slow starter who's done most recent racing over 7f and looked to find this trip stretching her a couple of times; capable if getting away and finding the race setting up for her but she's been expensive to follow in 0-50 company latterly.
runners.comment: Serial slow starter; she's been expensive to follow in 0-50 company latterly

runners.name: Ciotog
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner (good, soft), all in the autumn off higher marks; may just be on the downgrade but the handicapper's been quick to ease off and Billy Loughnane is back up for the first time this year.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner; handicapper's been quick to ease off and Billy Loughnane is back up

runners.name: Havana Club
runners.spotlight: Couple of ordinary efforts on the AW since unseating in February but he returns to turf on a career-low mark, 1lb lower than when winning over C&D last spring; the hood returning could help and he ought to fare better.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than when winning over C&D last spring; the hood returning could help

runners.name: Hawaiian King
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden (1m-1m4f) who's with his third yard already; has shown enough on the AW this year, however, to suggest he can play a part on today's turf debut, down another 3lb.
runners.comment: Showed enough on the AW this year to suggest he can play a part on turf debut, down 3lb
runners.owner: Aura Racing Dave Brown Joe Lane

runners.name: Villalobos
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who returns to turf 4lb higher than when last seen on it after a Polytrack win in the winter; 0-11 round here down the years and needs to bounce back from a lesser effort at Lingfield a month ago.
runners.comment: 0-11 round here down the years and needs to bounce back from a lesser AW effort

runners.name: Baynoona
runners.spotlight: C&D winner for Millie Wonnacott off a 3lb lower mark last autumn; heavy ground may have been behind a lesser effort here on her final start and she's one to consider on comeback.
runners.comment: C&D winner for Millie Wonnacott off 3lb lower last autumn; one to consider on comeback
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Miss Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Has not run on turf since 2023 and this is the first time she's ever gone beyond 7f; typically inconsistent since since eight-month layoff and it could well be all or nothing again.
runners.comment: Inconsistent since returning from eight months off; it  could well be all or nothing again

runners.name: Nammos
runners.spotlight: Debut for a seventh different yard having been beaten at short prices three times for James Owen this year, latterly in a visor (off today); not convincing in a few runs over this trip.
runners.comment: Beaten at short prices three times for James Owen this year; this trip stretches her

runners.name: Redhot Whisper
runners.spotlight: Highly tried on occasion by Ben Brookhouse and regressed sharply last year prior to being sold for Â£5,000 in October; big prices and well held in four AW runs for this yard, which also saddles Havana Club; hard to latch on to unless there's been market support behind him.
runners.comment: Big prices, well held for this yard; hard to latch on to unless there's market strength

runners.name: Cherry Hill
runners.spotlight: Seen only three times last year, running poorly on each, and fared no better on her comeback in January; underwent wind surgery since.
runners.comment: Fared no better on her comeback in January; has since undergone wind surgery

runners.name: Majestic Leo
runners.spotlight: String of poor efforts under both codes since picked up from Ralph Beckett; wind surgery in the winter didn't help improve things, likewise the first-time blinkers worn last time.
runners.comment: Wind surgery in the winter didn't improve things, likewise the first-time blinkers latest

races.name: Ella Bury Always In Our Hearts Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent filly who lost a shoe when behind Port Noir at Kempton last time; goes without cheekpieces again under Billy Loughnane, who's 3-17 for the yard after a few recent winners for them.
runners.comment: Inconsistent; goes without cheekpieces under Billy Loughnane, who's 3-17 for the yard

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Sole win during a long career came in a 0-50 classified event on the AW in February; string of generally poor turf efforts when trained in Ireland dampens enthusiasm as he comes back down in distance.
runners.comment: String of generally poor turf efforts when trained in Ireland dampens enthusiasm

runners.name: Rainbow Sign
runners.spotlight: Running fairly well on the AW when last seen but in only 0-50 company and he is a highly infrequent winner; no more than each-way claims.
runners.comment: Running fairly well on the AW but in only 0-50 company and he's a highly infrequent winner

runners.name: Judge Frank
runners.spotlight: Regressed last year and goes without the usual headgear on his return from four months off; twice ran well off higher marks over C&D last summer, though, and ran well on his 2yo debut, so a better showing wouldn't surprise as long as there's not much rain.
runners.comment: Twice ran well off higher marks over C&D last summer; bold showing wouldn't shock

runners.name: James Park Woods
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come on good to soft/soft ground in September, including over C&D last autumn off 6lb lower; record returning from a break down the years is far from inspiring.
runners.comment: All four wins have come on good to soft/soft ground in September; uninspiring record fresh

runners.name: Sharma dAmour
runners.spotlight: Won second time out last spring and a reproduction wouldn't surprise, as she was not suited by the way the race panned out on AW comeback; on a competitive mark now for a yard enjoying a good spell and she is one to consider.
runners.comment: Not suited by the way the race panned out on her AW comeback; one to consider

runners.name: Mishys Star
runners.spotlight: Exposed as ordinary on the AW, placed just once from ten starts; needs to improve a good deal for the switch to turf.
runners.comment: Exposed as ordinary on the AW; needs to improve a good deal for the switch to turf

runners.name: Pessoa
runners.spotlight: Frequent Wolverhampton winner who's 0-12 on turf, including in his Irish days (has 4lb lower mark than on the AW); very consistent, though, and should get the good pace he wants, so every chance he gets involved again.
runners.comment: Very consistent and should get the good pace he wants; every chance he gets involved again

runners.name: Port Noir
runners.spotlight: It's almost three years since she won on turf but she's struck up a good relationship with Rose Dawes on the AW this year, winning three of her last four; hard to discount in current form.
runners.comment: Struck up a good relationship with Rose Dawes on the AW; hard to discount in current form

runners.name: Bungle Bay
runners.spotlight: Wolverhampton specialist who doesn't run often on turf these days and rarely goes this far; not in much form on the AW this year and isn't obviously fancied.
runners.comment: Not in much form at his beloved Wolverhampton this year and isn't obviously fancied

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Didn't take to hurdles late last year having enjoyed a consistent time on the Flat; sole win came over C&D off today's mark and Rossa Ryan, who rode a winner for the yard at Doncaster last weekend, is an obvious plus.
runners.comment: Sole win came over C&D off today's mark and Rossa Ryan is an obvious plus

runners.name: Amathus
runners.spotlight: Often came to hand early in the past but is getting on now and has never won first time out; carries a big weight, ideally suited by an easy 7f and today's stiff 1m doesn't appeal as optimal.
runners.comment: Often came to hand early in the past; getting on now; stiff 1m doesn't appeal as optimal

runners.name: Red Admiral
runners.spotlight: Couple of costly defeats for George Boughey early last year, not looking the easiest; more of the same in three runs for his current yard and there are obvious risks back up in trip in a first-time hood after eight months off; likely has the ability though.
runners.comment: Risks involved in a first-time hood after eight months off but likely has the ability

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: Makes debut for a sixth yard, having closed out last season with a heavy-ground C&D win in a first-time tongue-tie for Alison Thorpe; there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh, and a 7lb rise looks plenty.
runners.comment: Debut for a sixth yard; nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; 7lb rise looks plenty

races.name: BetGoodwin Free Bet Nose Losers Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eremenko
runners.spotlight: Closely related to connections' smart hurdler Doddiethegreat; good-ground bumper winner who has performed okay in two runs over hurdles; latest conqueror at Chepstow (a stablemate of Pigeon Forge; 2m3f, soft) looks a nice prospect; clear claims.
runners.comment: Bumper winner and probably bumped into a nice prospect on second run over hurdles

runners.name: Risk De Cerisy
runners.spotlight: The form of her second in an Irish point is nothing special but she should leave that behind under rules having joined a top trainer who fits a tongue-tie; the market should be informative.
runners.comment: The form of her second in an Irish point is nothing special but she's joined a top yard

runners.name: Pigeon Forge
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who has finished a remote fifth on two visits to Hereford, in a soft-ground bumper and good-ground maiden hurdle; has to raise her game.
runners.comment: Remote fifth on two visits to Hereford, in a soft-ground bumper and good-ground hurdle

runners.name: That Is Blue
runners.spotlight: Only half a length behind a subsequent winner when third in a heavy-ground bumper at Wincanton; bred to do well as a jumper.
runners.comment: Only half a length behind a subsequent winner when third in a heavy-ground bumper

runners.name: Approaching Storm
runners.spotlight: Has no form claims and was 500-1 when tailed off in a C&D maiden last time.
runners.comment: Has no form claims and was 500-1 when tailed off in a C&D maiden last time

races.name: BetGoodwin Super Nigel Boardman Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Delgany Deadline
runners.spotlight: Has improved for cheekpieces, following his second at Sedgefield (3m2f, good to soft) with a 3l success at Leicester (2m6f, soft) which was despite a mistake two out; probably still fairly treated off 5lb higher.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester despite hitting two out and probably still fairly treated off 5lb higher

runners.name: Sweet Nightingale
runners.spotlight: 1-10 over hurdles and now 1-6 as a chaser after drawing clear for a 10l success at Exeter (3m, good); raised 7lb for winning that weak contest but there's no depth to this, either.
runners.comment: Raised 7lb for winning a weak race at Exeter but there's no depth to this, either.

runners.name: Ballyfinn
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and lengthy breaks are a feature in his profile; beaten at least 10l in all three runs over fences (2m5f-3m1f) and he needs to find a bit from somewhere to defy this sort of mark, for all that it's dropped.
runners.comment: Needs to find a bit from somewhere to defy this sort of mark, for all that it's dropped

runners.name: Milan Milos
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler who was never that threatening in handicaps but his breeding offers plenty of hope that he'll do better over fences and over this longer distance; tongue-tie added; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Breeding offers hope that he'll do better over fences and over this longer distance
runners.owner: Team Payne Powell Ray And Warburton

races.name: Charlotte Savill Memorial Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dreams Ka
runners.spotlight: Minor form so far, including when 40-1 for handicap debut at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft); top trainer fits a tongue-tie; would need to see greater confidence behind him.
runners.comment: Tongue-tie fitted after forgettable handicap debut, for which he went off at 40-1

runners.name: Shalfa
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner at an ordinary level; lightly raced hurdler who ran well for a long way on her handicap debut at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) and she'd approve should conditions be drier here.
runners.comment: Five Flat wins; encouraging handicap debut over hurdles; drier ground here would suit

runners.name: Pottersmattyeeehaa
runners.spotlight: Last run wasn't his best but likeable efforts prior to that in handicaps at about 2m3f (good to soft); has more pressing claims than most in here.
runners.comment: Last run wasn't his best but likeable efforts prior to that in handicaps at about 2m3f

runners.name: Goodwin Face
runners.spotlight: Hasn't always looked entirely convincing in a finish but he's only raced six times and has twice gone close at this track, in novice and handicap company; danger to all if fitness isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Has twice gone close at this venue and a danger to all if fitness isn't an issue

runners.name: Major Major
runners.spotlight: Won three on the Flat but doesn't look up to much over hurdles; completely tailed off on handicap debut here when friendless in the betting; tries two new forms of headgear.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off on handicap debut here when friendless in the betting

runners.name: Pink n Purple
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; only 6-1 for last month's handicap debut here but dropped away disappointingly; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Only 6-1 for last month's handicap debut here but dropped away disappointingly

runners.name: Voix De Bocelli
runners.spotlight: Last AW run was moderate but he'd been running well in that scene; beaten between 14l and 17l when registering three consecutive fourths over hurdles late last year and he's now 0-13 in this discipline; still entitled to each-way consideration.
runners.comment: 0-13 in this discipline but has been running well in defeat on the AW

runners.name: Definite
runners.spotlight: Sole career win came in 2022 when trained by Ralph Beckett; only minor promise over hurdles and hasn't offered all that much in this year's AW races after a long absence.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdler; hasn't offered all that much in this year's AW races after a long absence

runners.name: Ballybay
runners.spotlight: 9yo has raced six times under rules for three different yards; pulled up in both runs for this one, over hurdles and fences.
runners.comment: Raced six times under rules for three different yards; pulled up in both runs for this one

races.name: Harry & Betty Savill Memorial Handicap Chase

runners.name: Away For The Night
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden over hurdles; inherited second place on chase debut at Taunton (2m, good) but that was a decent first attempt as he wasn't beaten far; same mark for this.
runners.comment: Inherited second on chase debut at Taunton but that was still a decent first attempt

runners.name: Sea Thrift
runners.spotlight: Made all here in January and now 2-4 over fences after repeating the dose when odds-on in a weak three-runner race at Southwell (again on soft); 6lb higher for this but it's another modest contest and this drier ground should be fine.
runners.comment: Made all for both her wins over fences and looks mildly progressive; up 6lb for last time

runners.name: Angels Dream
runners.spotlight: 1-6 over fences, the win coming two seasons ago off 6lb higher (2m, soft); returned from a long absence in December and he's been a long way beneath that winning form in three races.
runners.comment: A long way beneath his winning form in three races after a long absence

runners.name: Pleasure Garden
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner and 1-10 over fences; peak efforts were a long time ago now and he was tailed off when third at Huntingdon after 297 days off; 4lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Tailed off when third at Huntingdon after 297 days off; 4lb out of the weights

races.name: BetGoodwin Sussex Champion Hurdle Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Indemnity
runners.spotlight: Very progressive in completing a good-ground hat-trick last year and when third to Tutti Quanti in a Class 1 handicap at Newbury in November (soft); still on a competitive mark despite all that and for whatever reason he didn't show his true form at Kempton on final sighting at Christmas; if forgiven that, and he's had a break, then he enters the picture.
runners.comment: Very progressive until his run over Christmas but he's had a lengthy break since

runners.name: Fasol
runners.spotlight: Has won at this trip but last four runs have been over further and he hasn't kicked on from his Musselburgh win in January (2m4f, good to soft; 3lb lower); probably has little margin for error mark-wise and unlikely the same is true about some of his rivals.
runners.comment: Little margin for error mark-wise and unlikely the same is true about some of his rivals
runners.owner: P J Vogt And Bryan Drew

runners.name: Nap Hand
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner; back from a break to finish fourth behind Hamlet's Night on the AW and then ran third of six in a Fakenham handicap hurdle; this race is much tougher than that one.
runners.comment: Third of eight at Fakenham last time and this race is much tougher than that one

runners.name: Soleil dArizona
runners.spotlight: All three hurdle wins have been on good ground so drying conditions are a positive, and she has had to deal with soft surfaces the last twice; however, this is by some way the strongest hurdle race she's contested to date.
runners.comment: Good ground would suit but this is the strongest hurdle race she's contested to date

runners.name: General Briar
runners.spotlight: Made all for wins here (2m novice, soft) and at Newbury (handicap, good to soft; 2lb lower) but he couldn't dominate when ultimately tailed off behind Mondo Man in the Imperial Cup (25-1); there are similar reservations as to how this will pan out, but Chris Gordon (doubly represented) has a very good record in this race with three wins since 2016.
runners.comment: Couldn't dominate when ultimately tailed off behind Mondo Man in the Imperial Cup

runners.name: Helnwein
runners.spotlight: Had strong handicap form even before finishing second to Alexei in the Greatwood and fifth in an Ascot handicap that could barely have worked out any better, with the four ahead of him all delivering high-profile efforts in the meantime (well in with fourth-placed Mondo Man); failed favourite backers in a lower grade at Newbury but this strong traveller might have won had he not been left in front two out (then idled); every chance should this pan out more favourably; all ground seems fine.
runners.comment: Has some classy handicap form and he looked unlucky not to win at Newbury last time

runners.name: Hamlets Night
runners.spotlight: Won twice early-season and better again when second in classy small-field races at Cheltenham and Wincanton, the latest a Grade 2; returned from a break with a win on the AW so his run in the Morebattle at Kelso goes down as disappointing; the return of a hood is no bad thing; enjoys good ground.
runners.comment: In excellent form until his no-show in the Morebattle; return of a hood is no bad thing

runners.name: Gentleman Joe
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat and over hurdles for Henry de Bromhead; his first run over jumps since 2023 when an encouraging fourth at Newbury, but hard to envisage him reversing those placings with Helnwein.
runners.comment: Fourth at Newbury and hard to envisage him reversing those placings with Helnwein

runners.name: Intellotto
runners.spotlight: Flat and jumps winner for Joseph O'Brien; it's not been an easy start for current yard, finishing last in the Grade 2 Kingwell (50-1) and then well down the field when 100-1 for the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; has something to prove all of a sudden.
runners.comment: Flat/jumps winner for Joseph O'Brien; tough tasks for this yard but hasn't shone at all

runners.name: Half A Chance
runners.spotlight: 1-14 over hurdles but placed a few times, including on his forays to Britain; he'll enjoy this ground more than what he faced in Ireland last time but it's a struggle to call him well handicapped.
runners.comment: Testing ground an excuse last time in Ireland; however, it's hard to call him well treated

runners.name: Serious Challenge
runners.spotlight: Has improved for patient tactics, winning at Haydock (1m7f, good) before going close in the always-strong Morebattle Hurdle at Kelso (good to soft); raised 3lb for that but this former smart Flat performer should be seen to good effect around here.
runners.comment: 2nd in the Morebattle; classy on the Flat and should be seen to good effect around here

runners.name: Ajs Diamond
runners.spotlight: It was against her own sex when breezing clear from off the pace at Warwick (2m, good) but, still lightly raced, she could be worthy of her 11lb rise in the weights; up in class but this race could be run to suit her style and she's of some interest.
runners.comment: Raised 11lb for winning so well at Warwick but could be worthy of her new mark

runners.name: Mondo Man
runners.spotlight: Initially disappointing over hurdles given how smart he was on the Flat, with his keen-going tendencies holding him back; however, a switch to front-running has yielded wins in a maiden here and the Imperial Cup at Sandown (10lb lower); both those came on soft ground but this faster surface won't bother him at all; they've found the key to him by the looks.
runners.comment: All the better for front-running, last time winning the Imperial Cup off 10lb lower

runners.name: Inside Man
runners.spotlight: Did well in juvenile/novice hurdles and he reappeared in September with a handicap win in a five-runner race at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good to soft; comfortably off 4lb lower); Newbury in November was disappointing but he did go off favourite and has had a break since; remains of interest.
runners.comment: 4-10 over hurdles; disappointing favourite last time but in November so has had a break

races.name: BetGoodwin Top Online Bookie Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Inca De Lafayette
runners.spotlight: Fine on all ground; well handicapped on his peak form over hurdles and has only raced twice as a chaser; no match for the winner at Exeter last time (2m1f) but that horse was favourite and unexposed; respected.
runners.comment: Well treated on hurdle form; 0-2 over fences; bumped into an improver last time

runners.name: Superstylin
runners.spotlight: Mostly in very good form since joining this yard and under a positive ride he held on comfortably when back from a break at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft), beating a reliable yardstick in the process; 4lb rise was fair; cheekpieces replace usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Mostly in good form since joining this yard and won well at Fontwell; raised a fair 4lb

runners.name: Sanitiser
runners.spotlight: Doing surprisingly well over fences as he's not bred for them; he might just be a shade high in the weights now after his hat-trick bid came up shy in a three-runner race but he must still have some sort of chance on ground he handles.
runners.comment: Perhaps a shade high in the weights now after his hat-trick bid came up shy

runners.name: Person Of Interest
runners.spotlight: Beaten 8l into third at Clonmel four starts ago (2m5f, good to yielding) but now has to bounce back after non-completions over fences and hurdles; has the most to prove of these.
runners.comment: Has the most to prove of these after non-completions over hurdles and fences
runners.owner: Anthony N Durkan & B M Durkan

races.name: BetGoodwin Master Trainer Chris Gordon Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Market House
runners.spotlight: Only 1-13 over hurdles and ran poorly on his last three attempts; however, better can be expected as he has been running well on the AW this year.
runners.comment: Last three jumps runs were poor; better can be expected as he's been running well on AW

runners.name: Washington Singer
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has hinted at winning potential but last run at Taunton (3m, good) was a backward step as he didn't look straightforward; if the cheekpieces can focus his mind then the ability is there to take a hand.
runners.comment: Maiden; if the cheekpieces can focus his mind then the ability is there to take a hand

runners.name: Rukaana
runners.spotlight: Arrives here on the back of three very solid runs, winning at Windsor (2m, good) and going down narrowly since on soft and good ground; off the same mark as last time and it's just a question of whether he'll stay this far (Flat bred).
runners.comment: In a good run of form and it's just a question of whether he'll stay this far (Flat bred)

runners.name: Eliza Dolittle
runners.spotlight: Course regular who patently failed to give her true running here last time when much shorter in the betting than runner-up Ephyra; did it easily from the front over C&D the time before when 7lb lower; solid chance if back on song.
runners.comment: Last run here was too bad to be true; won easily the time before; chance if back on song

runners.name: Ahellofaman
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on chase debut last time; hasn't had much racing and he was second in a Fairyhouse handicap hurdle in February (2m, heavy), albeit some 12l off the winner; all his form is in the mud, except when well beaten in a good-ground bumper.
runners.comment: Second in an Irish handicap hurdle; chasing last time; all his best form is in the mud

runners.name: Path Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in handicaps from 2m to 2m5f; probably failed to stay nearly 3m last time and her previous run at Fakenham wasn't too shoddy; 2lb out of the weights but could play some role here.
runners.comment: Placed in handicaps from 2m to 2m5f; probably failed to stay nearly 3m last time

runners.name: Ephyra
runners.spotlight: Placed on Flat (2m AW) for David Menuisier; lightly raced over hurdles and hasn't been far away in 2m/2m4f handicaps here on last two outings; latest conqueror was very well backed; considered.
runners.comment: Lightly raced over hurdles and hasn't been far away in 2m/2m4f handicaps here

runners.name: Smith
runners.spotlight: Had lots of Flat racing and won eight times; has yet to offer a great deal over jumps (0-7) but soft ground was no good to him here last time; others are still far more convincing.
runners.comment: Soft ground was no good to him last time but others are still far more convincing

races.name: GB Civil Engineering Handicap

runners.name: Andalprofit
runners.spotlight: Sole AW success came at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) a year ago and was only beaten a neck having attempted to make all back there 15 days ago; not the most consistent, though, and well held in both previous goes over C&D.
runners.comment: Best AW form has come at Newcastle; not very consistent and well held in two goes over C&D

runners.name: Tuscan Point
runners.spotlight: Has done well in two starts since joining Adam Kirby with this headgear combination, winning easily on his return from 149 days off over C&D last month and only going down by half a length back here ten days later following a 10lb rise; much respected from the same mark.
runners.comment: First and second over C&D last month since joining Adam Kirby; much respected

runners.name: Starsong
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who made a fine return from 129 days off when beaten a neck by a subsequent winner here last month (Beelzebub an unlucky fourth); only 1lb higher and should go well again.
runners.comment: C&D winner and only beaten a neck on last month's return; should go well again

runners.name: Fenlander
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times, including in two starts under positive rides over C&D in November, but now 0-13 and he may need this after 135 days off.
runners.comment: Placed a few times but now 0-13 and may need this after 135 days off

runners.name: Neyvas Angel
runners.spotlight: Close third of six on her Yarmouth debut (6f, good to soft) last May, but she hasn't built on it and now 0-8, the hood making little difference last time; hard to recommend back in trip.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on a promising debut and now 0-8; hard to recommend back in trip

runners.name: Melissa Honey
runners.spotlight: 0-11 and ended last season with two modest efforts; returns from five months off and stable fields a much stronger candidate in Good Earth.
runners.comment: 0-11 and ended last season with two modest efforts; returns from five months off

runners.name: Good Earth
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-15 under Connor Planas when gaining his second C&D success ten days ago; did it comfortably enough and despite a 5lb higher mark, is a major player if in the same form; stable also runs Melissa Honey.
runners.comment: Won comfortably over C&D ten days ago; major player despite a 5lb higher mark

runners.name: Beelzebub
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 19, but he has dropped a long way in the weights and met all sorts of trouble when fourth of 11 over C&D last time (Starsong second); withdrawn at the start on vet's advice in the race won by Good Earth here ten days ago; worth considering.
runners.comment: Has dropped a long way in the weights and caught the eye when fourth here last time

runners.name: Daytona Lady
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner on the AW including two over C&D, but losing run up to 16 despite a slipping mark; not seen in 162 days and probably best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, but losing run up to 16 and not seen in 162 days; probably best watched
runners.owner: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr

races.name: Heggs Family Handicap

runners.name: French Affair
runners.spotlight: Consistent since narrowly making a successful nursery debut at Newbury (6f, good) last August, including when beaten a head at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) 18 days ago on his return from six months off; 2lb higher but has fewer questions to answer than most of these; major player.
runners.comment: Beaten a head at Kempton 18 days ago on his return from six months off; major player

runners.name: Wetsand
runners.spotlight: In good form in her early starts on turf last year, including chasing home Monday's Burradon Stakes winner Timeforshowcasing in a valuable event at Goodwood (7f, good to soft); has found life tougher in warm company since, though, and has enough to prove on AW/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Started off well last year, but has found life tougher since; makes AW/handicap debut

runners.name: Loblolly
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose form on AW last year read 221 as opposed to 686 on turf; returns from 192 days off having been gelded and the market should be interesting.
runners.comment: Form on AW last year read 221 as opposed to 686 on turf; watch market after 192 days off

runners.name: Henry Halflight
runners.spotlight: All three starts at Newcastle; narrowly getting off the mark in a slowly run 7f novice last month (runner-up Blue Mantle runs in the 3.06); has more on his plate on this handicap debut, but he remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Won a 7f novice at Newcastle last month; has more on his plate, but remains unexposed

runners.name: Colori Forever
runners.spotlight: Ran well in all three starts last year, making a successful debut at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) and going down by half a length to a subsequent winner in a four-runner Doncaster nursery (7f, heavy) on her final outing; out of an AW winner and worth a second look if ready to go after 162 days off.
runners.comment: Ran well in all three starts last year; out of an AW winner and worth a second look

runners.name: Gold Queen Kindly
runners.spotlight: Justified skinny odds on his Pontefract debut (6f, good to firm) last August and ran well on turf afterwards, but having been gelded has been well held in two starts on the AW since returning in January; needs a resurgence over the extra furlong.
runners.comment: Well held in two starts on AW since returning in January; needs a resurgence upped to 7f

runners.name: Just A Girl
runners.spotlight: Bolted up from the front in a Lingfield novice (7.6f, good) last August, but then well held in a York nursery and Newmarket Listed event; closely related to AW winners, but not seen in five months and not quite sure how well handicapped she is.
runners.comment: Not seen in five months and not quite sure how well handicapped she is

runners.name: Come On Eibhlin
runners.spotlight: Landed a Windsor novice (6f, good) last summer but faced impossible tasks in most of her other starts; probably found the trip too sharp when fifth of nine on nursery debut here (5f; first-time blinkers) in October, but still has enough to prove back from 163 days off.
runners.comment: Has faced some impossible tasks; has enough to prove back from 163 days off
runners.owner: Hold My Beer Gee Gees Racing & N Clark

races.name: Grace & Dotty Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Receipt
runners.spotlight: Best effort came in his only start for Pam Sly when second of eight in a similar race over C&D in February, but not so good back with his former trainer at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) 11 days ago; return here may help, but this still looks a tough task against the 3yos.
runners.comment: Best effort when runner-up over C&D in February but this looks tough against the 3yos

runners.name: Blue Mantle
runners.spotlight: Fair third of seven on his Hamilton debut (6f, good to firm) last May, but not seen again until beaten a short head on last month's Newcastle return (7f, Tapeta); that race was slowly run but the form still makes him a major player in this.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head on last month's Newcastle return; that form makes him a major player

runners.name: Back To Me
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut when narrowly winning a Dundalk median auction maiden (6f, Polytrack) for Adrian Murray in November; makes his debut for his new stable after five months off having been gelded; has more to do under his penalty, but the market should be revealing.
runners.comment: Won narrowly at Dundalk in November; stable debut after five months off; market revealing

runners.name: Different Opinions
runners.spotlight: Sent off 12-1 when second of six on last month's Chelmsford debut (7f, Polytrack); bred more for sprint trips, but still high on the list if coming on from that first performance.
runners.comment: Runner-up on last month's Chelmsford debut; high on the list if coming on from that effort

runners.name: You Will See
runners.spotlight: 26,000gns foal; first foal; dam 1m winner (RPR 84), half-sister to winners Azure Blue (6f Group 2) and Dapper Guest (7f), out of unraced close relative of 1m Group 3 winner Pincheck; has been gelded and undergone wind surgery before this debut; stable not known for winning newcomers.
runners.comment: Has been gelded and undergone wind surgery; stable not known for winning newcomers

runners.name: Niffler
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; brother to German 8.5f winner Lassair, half-brother to German winners Lacento (7.5f/1m) and Lionel (1m1f/1m2f); dam 8.4f winner (RPR 89), closely related to German winners La Saldana (1m Group 3) and La Dawa (Listed-placed 7f 2yo); already gelded; worth a market check, albeit the stable's last two successful newcomers were sent off at 28-1 and 16-1.
runners.comment: Already gelded, but still worth a market check

runners.name: Vicit Venture
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal; closely related to 5f/6f 2yo winner Educating Rita (RPR 85); dam placed 5f 2yo (67), half-sister to winners Dutch Kingdom (useful 6f-8.6f) and Seas Of Wells (6f; dam of 1m 2yo Group 2 winner Cadillac); sports plenty of headgear on debut, but stable had a well-backed winning newcomer in a similar contest over C&D in February; well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Stable had a winning newcomer in a similar contest over C&D in February; watch market

runners.name: Jimmys Army
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros yearling; dam 1m winner (RPR 76), half-sister to useful 1m3f-2m winner Rare Groove, out of French 5.5f 2yo winner; already gelded and stable doesn't have many winning newcomers.
runners.comment: Already gelded and stable doesn't have many winning newcomers

runners.name: Cobalt Comet
runners.spotlight: Astronomical prices when out of the frame in both starts over 1m here; second performance was a bit better than the first, but he still looks one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in both starts over 1m here; looks one for handicaps after this

runners.name: Kode Grey
runners.spotlight: 25-1 when beating one home on his Newcastle debut (6f, Tapeta) in February; looks one for handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Beat one home on his Newcastle debut in February; looks one for handicaps in due course

races.name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: City Queen
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in both starts since returning in February, firstly behind Pendella at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in February and then behind a nice newcomer over this C&D last month; ought to be bang there once again.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both starts since returning in February; ought to be bang there once again

runners.name: Pendella
runners.spotlight: Only made her debut in December and got off the mark when beating City Queen by just under 2l (pair clear) in a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f, Tapeta) in February; only 1lb worse off and makes plenty of appeal, especially with the possibility of more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Beat City Queen by just under 2l in a Wolverhampton maiden in February; only 1lb worse off

runners.name: Likeness
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two small-field nurseries at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta) in the autumn; fair fourth of ten on last month's Chelmsford return (7f, Polytrack), but more will be needed if she is to win this.
runners.comment: Fair fourth of ten on last month's Chelmsford return, but more will be needed to win this

runners.name: Selection
runners.spotlight: Has proved consistent in AW handicaps (1534522) and was only beaten a neck in first-time cheekpieces at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta) 15 days ago; another 2lb rise not the end of the world, but she is up against some less-exposed types.
runners.comment: Has proved consistent in AW handicaps (1534522), but is up against some less-exposed types

runners.name: Kiss Me My Love
runners.spotlight: 2-18, so much the most exposed in the field and she failed to beat a rival on her Wolverhampton return (8.6f, Tapeta) 16 days ago; down the list.
runners.comment: Much the most exposed in the field and failed to beat a rival on her Wolverhampton return

runners.name: Song N Dance
runners.spotlight: Unexposed for a 4yo after just six starts and she ended last season on a high with wins in a Leicester novice (7f, good) and a Newcastle handicap (1m, Tapeta); now 7lb higher and is the only one in the field without a run this year.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two wins, but is now 7lb higher and lacks a recent run

races.name: Country & Western Night 25th April Handicap

runners.name: Dream Illusion
runners.spotlight: Proved a disappointing favourite after having got worked up in the stalls at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta) nine days ago, but she is 2-2 over this C&D and just 2lb above her last winning mark; a player if bouncing back.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D; just 2lb above last winning mark; player if rebounding from poor run latest

runners.name: Marry The Night
runners.spotlight: Ran perfectly well when third of 11 behind a subsequent winner at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) 16 days ago, but should relish the return here with his record over C&D reading 2211511; high on list.
runners.comment: Should relish the return here with his record over C&D reading 2211511; high on list

runners.name: Holly Mist
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three starts, though a slowly run race may not have suited her when third of five at Kempton (7f, Polytrack; 5-4) last time; up a furlong for her handicap debut which should be within range, but she is minus a hood for the first time.
runners.comment: Placed in all three starts; up a furlong for her handicap debut; no hood today

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Was back off his last winning mark when narrowly successful in first-time cheekpieces at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) in February, but finished behind Dream Illusion when fourth of eight at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta) ten days later; acts here, but still needs a bit more.
runners.comment: Behind Dream Illusion when fourth at Newcastle last time; acts here, but needs a bit more

runners.name: Hachiman
runners.spotlight: Won a Lingfield maiden (1m, Polytrack) for the Crisfords last autumn, but well held in all four handicaps including two for this yard; best watched.
runners.comment: Well held in all four handicaps including two for this yard; best watched
runners.owner: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Soames Forsyte
runners.spotlight: Has become inconsistent but has won four times here, the latest over this trip in January; just 1lb higher and a player if on a going day.
runners.comment: Has won four times here, the latest over this trip in January; a player if on a going day

runners.name: Looks Fantastic
runners.spotlight: 0-9; best effort when second of 12 in a maiden over C&D for Ed Bethell a year ago, but has regressed since including in three starts for this yard; needs to have derived benefit from a 107-day absence.
runners.comment: Best effort when runner-up in a maiden over C&D a year ago but he has regressed since

runners.name: American State
runners.spotlight: Returned from wind surgery with a success at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in February, but had excuses when only sixth of 11 there last month (wide draw/slow pace); has finished out of the frame in three visits here, though.
runners.comment: Had excuses at Wolverhampton last time, but out of the frame in three previous visits here

races.name: Book The Bistro In Seasons Restaurant Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: John Galt
runners.spotlight: Made debut in only December and ability on all four starts, best form when fifth of ten on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f) last time; tongue-tie and cheekpieces are now enlisted; form chance but probably vulnerable.
runners.comment: Form chance after latest start (1m4f, Tapeta) but probably vulnerable; equipment goes on

runners.name: Desert Belle
runners.spotlight: Well related, including as close relation to 1m-1m2f winner Dubai Icon (including AW; RPR 111); 10-11 for maiden at Lingfield (1m, AW) 16 days ago, looking sluggish at most stages but she ran on into second of eight; handed an extra 4f today and she's a leading player on form and potential.
runners.comment: 10-11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) 16 days ago, sluggish at most stages but running on into 2nd

runners.name: Pure Moon
runners.spotlight: 35,000gns yearling by Sea The Moon; 18-1 and slowly into stride for novice at Nottingham (1m, heavy) last October but he gradually worked his way forward to emerge in second of 12; swiftly raised to 1m4f and brings clear potential, so he looks a leading contender.
runners.comment: 18-1 and slowly into stride at Nottingham (1m, heavy) in October but emerged in 2nd of 12
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Lvii & Mrs G Jarvis

runners.name: Joyful Lawyer
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts on Tapeta late last year; gelded and first-time headgear when showing more for fourth of six from the front in a maiden at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) four weeks ago; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Gelded and first-time headgear when showing more last time (1m2f); needs to build on that

runners.name: Mystic Music
runners.spotlight: 33-1 in a maiden at Chelmsford (1m, AW) last month and beaten 20l; at least her sire and siblings suggest this will be more her trip.
runners.comment: 33-1 for maiden at Chelmsford (1m, AW) last month and beaten 20l; seems bred more for 1m4f

runners.name: Poppeye
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Expert Eye half-brother to winners Real Terms (1m2f-1m4f including AW; RPR 86) and Termonator (6f/1m AW; 68); dam 9.7f-1m4f winner (100) out of unraced half-sister to Irish St Leger winner Sans Frontieres; it would be a bit surprising if debut is his day.
runners.comment: Expert Eye half-brother to two winners (one with RPR 86); dam 9.7f-1m4f winner (100)

runners.name: Havanas Tune
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous when beaten 16l or so at Southwell (1m) and Kempton (1m4f) this year; wore cheekpieces both starts, plus tongue-tie on latest; now tries blinkers; no form claims yet.
runners.comment: Never dangerous, beaten 16l or so at Southwell (1m) and Kempton (1m4f); different headgear

runners.name: The Lakes
runners.spotlight: Big prices, soundly beaten in her three bumpers and a C&D novice.
runners.comment: Big prices, soundly beaten in her three bumpers and a C&D novice

runners.name: Here Comes Louie
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when well beaten in a novice at Redcar (1m, good) in October and 80-1 when even further adrift in a maiden here (1m, AW) one week later; pedigree is not a strong pointer to this trip.
runners.comment: Big prices when well beaten twice over 1m in October

races.name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Jack Andrea
runners.spotlight: Took him 19 attempts to break his duck but his last three efforts read 121, the latest win coming by a head over 1m3f here eight days ago; 3lb rise fair enough and should stay the extra furlong; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Last three efforts read 121, including a win over 1m3f here eight days ago; shortlisted
runners.owner: J Rayment J Stephenson & R Eddery

runners.name: Late Claim
runners.spotlight: Mainly performed well after making a successful handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, Polytrack) in December, but needs to put a lesser effort over C&D 15 days ago behind her; first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Needs to put a lesser effort over C&D 15 days ago behind her; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Silkies Sib
runners.spotlight: Consistent after Boxing Day (1332213) prior to a lesser performance at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) last time (pulled hard); has run well over 1m3f here, but efforts over this far leave something to be desired.
runners.comment: Mainly consistent since Boxing Day but efforts over this far leave something to be desired

runners.name: Arths Gold
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but placed in two of his three starts over 1m3f here, on both occasions when sporting a new type of headgear; each-way claims again based on those efforts.
runners.comment: 0-11 but placed in two of his three starts over 1m3f here; each-way claims

runners.name: Heartened
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 16 starts came at Newcastle (1m2f, Tapeta) last September; still only 1lb higher but has stamina to prove on her first attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: 1-16; has stamina to prove on her first attempt at this trip

runners.name: Backer Bilk
runners.spotlight: Makes his debut for his fourth different stable (same owners) on his 11th outing, having been off since finishing lame at Chelmsford last August; has both his stamina and wellbeing to prove.
runners.comment: Debut for fourth different stable after seven months off; stamina and wellbeing to prove

runners.name: Lednikov
runners.spotlight: All six AW wins have come here (four of them under Elle-May Croot), the latest by more than 7l over 1m6f last month; has been hit with an 8lb rise, but could enjoy the run of the race if reverting to his usual front-running tactics.
runners.comment: All six AW wins have come here; hit with an 8lb rise, but could enjoy the run of the race

runners.name: Yakhabar
runners.spotlight: Three wins on the AW, the latest a 4l success over C&D last month; 6lb rise will demand more but still respected if maintaining his form; visor returns.
runners.comment: Won easily over C&D last time; 6lb higher, but still respected if maintaining his form

runners.name: Easter Sundae
runners.spotlight: 0-13; first placing when beaten just over a length into second of nine in first-time cheekpieces at Newcastle (1m4f, Tapeta; 100-1) seven weeks ago; remains to be seen if the form can be repeated.
runners.comment: 0-13; runner-up at Newcastle seven weeks ago; remains to be seen if form can be repeated

runners.name: Sycamore Gap
runners.spotlight: Sent off 1-3 when making it 17th-time lucky at Newcastle (1m4f, Tapeta) in September 2024; has since left Rebecca Menzies (same owners) and the market should reveal what is expected.
runners.comment: Makes his stable debut after 558 days off; market should reveal what is expected

races.name: Live Racing Streaming At CopyBet Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tread Carefully
runners.spotlight: Clear runner-up behind useful rivals on two of his five hurdling starts; unseated rider when short-odds favourite last time but has a big role to play if all goes well on this first venture beyond 2m.
runners.comment: Second to useful rivals twice over hurdles; big player if this new trip suits

runners.name: Emerald Breeze
runners.spotlight: Fair form when placed on all three hurdling starts, most recently when well-held third behind very useful rival at Chepstow (2m, soft) in February; should be okay on the faster ground here and is bred to be suited by the step up in trip; respected.
runners.comment: Fair form when placed on all three 2m hurdle starts; bred to be suited by this new trip

runners.name: Ade Boy
runners.spotlight: Won big-field Irish bumper in September and showed clear promise on both hurdling starts for new stable, more recently when third of 15 at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month; probably needs to improve here but that's possible if the new trip suits.
runners.comment: Irish bumper winner; looked promising on both 2m hurdle starts; up in trip today

runners.name: Exiguus
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in two course bumpers last year or when sixth of eight over C&D on hurdling debut three weeks ago but remains best watched.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on last month's hurdling debut but remains best watched

runners.name: Destination Unkown
runners.spotlight: Poor Flat maiden who was pulled up when 200-1 for hurdling debut here (2m, good to soft) on Boxing Day.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 200-1 for hurdling debut here (2m) on Boxing Day

races.name: Mariner Packaging Sing When We're Fishing Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: By The Grace
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at ninth attempt, keeping on well for Daniel Williams at Hereford (2m3f, good) last month; only 3lb higher here and further progress is possible; respected.
runners.comment: Came good at Hereford last month, keeping on well, and is only 3lb higher here

runners.name: Lillistar
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles, having failed to complete her last two races in this sphere, but made the frame three consecutive times in the autumn (2m4f-2m5f, good-soft) and recently ran well on the Flat; considered each-way.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles but made the frame three times in autumn and recently ran well on Flat

runners.name: Fine As Wine
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on last two starts, more recently when third of 12 on handicap debut at Southwell (2m4f, good) 12 days ago; competes off same mark here; no surprise if she takes another step forward.
runners.comment: Third of 12 on recent handicap debut at Southwell; no surprise if she features here

runners.name: Perfect Arch
runners.spotlight: 0-31 over hurdles and 0-51 overall; has shown some bits and bobs of form this season and is suited by this C&D but still makes only limited appeal from 5lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Suited by this C&D but is 0-51 overall and competes from 5lb out of the weights

runners.name: Blue Bear
runners.spotlight: Won over fences in 2024 (2m1f, soft) but soundly beaten in a hurdle race and two chases since returning from long absence in February; very unexposed over this sort of trip but needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in a hurdle and two chases since returning from long absence in February

runners.name: Raincloud
runners.spotlight: Struggled to get competitive in second half of last year and returns from break with overall strike-rate standing at 0-35; 4lb wrong; overlooked.
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive in second half of last year and is now 0-35 overall

runners.name: Inlet
runners.spotlight: 0-14 on the Flat; showed first glimmer of hurdling promise for new stable when fifth in C&D maiden three weeks ago but needs further improvement on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed some promise in recent C&D maiden; needs further improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: Gms Prince
runners.spotlight: 11yo who belatedly opened his account here (2m2f, good) in September, his 21st start over hurdles, and has remained in good form since; probably won't be far away but younger rivals have more potential.
runners.comment: Has remained in good form since course win in September but younger rivals appeal more

runners.name: De Deli Counter
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but ran ran big race from the front when close/clear second over C&D (good to soft) in early February and returns here after pretty good third at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) last month; visored for recent runs; could be hard to catch if he responds well to first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles but ran big race in defeat over C&D in February; headgear switched

runners.name: Melosa
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on the Flat last year (best efforts were in France) but was remote when third in Doncaster maiden hurdle (2m, good to soft) last month and needs to take a good step forward on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Remote when third in Doncaster novice last month; needs a good step forward here

races.name: CopyBet Supporting UK Horse Racing Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ballynaheer
runners.spotlight: In excellent form on good ground in the autumn, winning three times over fences (3m1f-3m2f); well behind Dangerous Touch when reappearing in four-runner race at Huntingdon last month but might have needed that outing and his usual cheekpieces are refitted here.
runners.comment: Three chase wins in the autumn; might have needed recent run; cheekpieces back on today

runners.name: Dangerous Touch
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over fences but placed three times; headed only in closing stages after running big race from the front at Huntingdon (2m7f, good; first-time blinkers) last month and has obvious claims if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Placed in three of six chases, succumbing only in closing stages at Huntingdon last time

runners.name: Achille Des Rocs
runners.spotlight: 2-4 over hurdles last season, with both wins gained on good ground (2m/2m3f); probably still has potential but was just a respectable fifth when upped to 2m7f for chasing debut at Kelso (good; first-time cheekpieces) last April and this is his first run since.
runners.comment: 2-4 over hurdles; made respectable chase debut last April but not seen since

runners.name: Rodney Bay
runners.spotlight: Just 1-24 over hurdles when based in Ireland and was pulled up (albeit on slow ground) on chasing debut in February; ran well to a point when back over hurdles for new stable ten days ago (first-time cheekpieces) but was well held in the end; others look safer.
runners.comment: Ran well to a point over hurdles on recent stable debut but was well held in the end

races.name: First Past The Post At CopyBet Handicap Chase

runners.name: Beat The Edge
runners.spotlight: On a good mark now (even from 3lb out of the weights) and arrested his decline with fairly good third of seven here (2m1f, good to soft; first-time hood) three weeks ago; however, this quirky customer doesn't always find much when the chips are down and has a modest strike-rate.
runners.comment: Ran quite well over C&D last month but doesn't always find much when the chips are down

runners.name: Lone Soldier
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over fences and 0-18 overall; clear second to an improver over C&D (soft) in January and now only 1lb above that mark but hasn't quite been in the same form since; cheekpieces removed after one use.
runners.comment: Clear second over C&D in January but not quite in same form since

runners.name: Cawthorne Cracker
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins; went close to a breakthrough chase success at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last September but absent since respectable hurdle run the following month and may be reliant upon genuine good ground.
runners.comment: Went close over fences in September; off since October; genuine good ground suits best

runners.name: Starman
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good form when close second to short-odds favourite at Catterick (1m7f, good) on New Year's Day, his third start for Joanne Foster, but pulled up there twice since and now has it all to prove again; tongue-tie (worn on penultimate start) refitted.
runners.comment: Close second at Catterick on New Year's Day but pulled up twice there since

runners.name: Kally Des Bruyeres
runners.spotlight: Scored off reduced hurdle mark at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft; first run after wind op) last month but was unable to justify favouritism when only fourth of six on chasing debut at Taunton (2m, good) a few days later, and needs a better performance here.
runners.comment: Won off reduced hurdle mark last month; only fourth of six on chase debut soon afterwards

runners.name: Forsa Bay
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising seasonal/chase debut over C&D in December and bounced back from a poor run with good third under Ellie Callwood at Hereford (2m, good; first-time visor) last month; could still have a bigger run in him; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Good third at Hereford last month and still lightly raced over fences; shortlisted

runners.name: Gower Prince
runners.spotlight: Absent since pulled up over hurdles for Matt Sheppard last summer (wore cheekpieces) but was unlucky not to win a chase for him at Chepstow (2m, good) just under a year ago, and he's now 5lb below that mark; will need to be taken very seriously if strong in the betting before this stable debut; usual hood refitted.
runners.comment: Absent since pulled up over hurdles last summer but makes stable debut from a good mark

runners.name: Its Party Time
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on first two hurdling starts and probably didn't achieve a great deal when last of four on his latest outing; showed promise in Irish points last year, though, and remains very unexposed under rules; worth a glance in the betting before chase/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Very unexposed under rules; worth market check before chase/handicap debut

races.name: Best Odds Guaranteed Overnight At CopyBet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Diesel Line
runners.spotlight: Dual winner on good ground at Uttoxeter last summer; still in good form when last seen in the autumn and probably still on a workable mark but has fitness to prove after four months off and recent rain reduces his appeal.
runners.comment: Did well in early part of season; back from a break today; best on genuine good ground

runners.name: Cawthorne Banker
runners.spotlight: Needed considerable help from the handicapper before winning twice last year and has looked badly out of sorts on both appearances this year; usual cheekpieces (left off last time) are refitted; can't be supported with any confidence.
runners.comment: Won twice in 2025 but has looked badly out of sorts on both appearances this year

runners.name: Kalkaroo
runners.spotlight: Battled gamely to reward good market support over C&D (soft) in February and has made the frame on both starts since, latterly when back here (good to soft) three weeks ago; strong contender again.
runners.comment: Twice in the frame since game C&D win in February; strong contender again

runners.name: Bouboule
