runners.spotlight: 0-16 under all codes; didn't run badly when fifth over hurdles at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in February but suffered a heavy defeat on AW last month and makes only limited appeal.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly on latest hurdling start but well beaten on Flat since; now 0-16 overall

runners.name: Bunker Bay
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but his third at Musselburgh (1m7f, soft) in December was a good effort and he stuck to his task pretty well when filling same position (about 4l behind Kalkaroo) over C&D (good to soft) last month.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles but stuck to his task pretty well when third over C&D last month

runners.name: Hijo De La Luna
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by first-time visor when winning basement-grade AW race in February but was below that form on latest Flat start, and this is his first hurdle run since he was pulled up here (2m4f, good) on handicap debut last June; no headgear today; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won basement-grade AW race in February but overall profile not persuasive

runners.name: Macho Sun
runners.spotlight: Last of five here (2m2f, good to soft) three weeks ago and now 0-27 under all codes; effectively on same mark from out of the weights today; blinkers (worn once on the Flat in 2024) are given another try.
runners.comment: Last of five here three weeks ago and now 0-27 under all codes

runners.name: Ruler Legend
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on first two starts for Gary Hanmer but his fifth of 11 at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) a fortnight ago was much more encouraging; could have a say if able to build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on first two stable starts but recent Carlisle fifth was much better

runners.name: Susiesparkle
runners.spotlight: Doubled her Flat tally with low-grade AW win in February 2025 but has poor record over hurdles and on the Flat since last summer; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Has poor record over hurdles and on the Flat since last summer

races.name: Safer Gambling At CopyBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kildare dIrlande
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who made good start to rules career when second of nine at Huntingdon (good) last month, having made the running under Rian Corcoran; likely contender again here.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; made good start to rules career when second at Huntingdon last month

runners.name: Buck Whaley
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all three Irish points last spring but showed improvement when fourth on most recent outing (good ground); subsequently changed hands for Â£26,000 and was given a wind op; wears a tongue-tie on rules debut; new stable does pretty well in bumpers.
runners.comment: Improved form when fourth in latest Irish point; bought for Â£26,000 last spring

runners.name: Clova Chocolate
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to connections' C&D bumper winner Simply Rouge, out of 2m4f hurdle winning half-sister to stable's Group 2 Flat/useful hurdle winner Clever Cookie; presence of headgear unnerving.
runners.comment: Had the right type of pedigree for bumpers but presence of headgear is unnerving on debut
runners.owner: Clova Farms And P D Niven

runners.name: Northerly
runners.spotlight: Â£18,000 3yo; fifth foal; half-brother to low-grade 3m/3m1f hurdle/chase winner Don Brocco; dam placed in bumpers/hurdles, half-sister to useful jumpers Bob Bob Bobbin and War General; wears headgear on debut; stable not known for bumper winners.
runners.comment: Wears headgear on debut; stable not known for bumper winners

runners.name: Rene El Loco
runners.spotlight: 23,000euros 3yo; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Away To Milan (2m1f hurdle) and Anyoneforsnacks (point); dam unraced sister to useful bumper/jumps winner Ceasar Milan; worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Out of a sister to a useful bumper winner; worth a glance in the betting

runners.name: Black Eddy
runners.spotlight: Impressed when winning readily on debut at Chepstow (good) for Toby Bulgin in October; tailed off (reportedly stopped quickly) when upped to Listed level at Ascot (soft) for new stable in December but was given a wind op afterwards; back in calmer waters and on better ground here; big player if his problems are behind him.
runners.comment: Impressed on debut; clearly not himself when struggling in Listed company; can bounce back

races.name: Osaka Hai (Grade 1) (Turf)
Santa Anita Derby (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Intrepido
runners.spotlight: 9-1 winner of Grade 1 American Pharoah Stakes here over 8.5f last October, surging late as leaders tired; second in Grade 3 Robert B. Lewis in February (duelled throughout with Plutarch, beaten under 1l); slight doubt whether he wants 1m1f (can race freely) but he should give another good account.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner over 8.5f and good second in Grade 3 since; claims, if seeing out 1m1f well
runners.owner: Dutch Girl Holdings LLC & Irving Ventures LLC

runners.name: Robusta
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner here in January but much improved form in blinkers when head second to Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe Stakes (8.5f) here last month, albeit well held by winner late on; should stay 1m1f and fair prospects of a place.
runners.comment: Best effort head second to Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe here; others have more scope

runners.name: Cherokee Nation
runners.spotlight: Big, awkward-running colt (pulls hard); flashed lots of talent in first five starts, but only fifth in two Graded events; scored by 10l in 1m maiden here in February, close to strong pace and opening up in great style, clocking very fast time; big player, if the penny has dropped mentally.
runners.comment: Big, awkward sort; improved when 10l winner of maiden here; dangerous if confirming that
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Potente
runners.spotlight: $2.4m yearling; unbeaten here in 6f maiden in January (made all) and Grade 2 San Felipe Stakes (8.5f) last month, in the latter chasing leaders while short of room and keeping on strongly to beat Robusta by a head once switched (went well clear after the line); still looked green and sure to improve again at 1m1f.
runners.comment: $2.4m yearling who won 8.5f Grade 2 San Felipe here on second start; the one to beat

runners.name: So Happy
runners.spotlight: Overcame some trouble to win Grade 2 San Vicente over 7f here in January; good third to Potente and Robusta, no extra late on, when third in Grade 2 San Felipe over 8.5f; step up to 1m1f does not appear in his favour.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner at 7f here, then outstayed in similar event over 8.5f; doubtful at 1m1f
runners.owner: Norman Stables Llc And Saints Or Sinners

runners.name: Start The Ride
runners.spotlight: 14-1 when winning valuable state-bred event here in January, well on top late on; well-beaten sixth (slowly away) behind Potente in Grade 2 San Vicente last time; must prove he belongs at this level after last time but should not be written off yet; needs to break better.
runners.comment: Won valuable state-bred event but well behind in Grade 2 here last time; could bounce back

runners.name: Vitruvian Man
runners.spotlight: Won 8.5f maiden at Keeneland last October; fairly useful form also on other starts, as when just under 3l third in allowance at Turfway Park (Tapeta) last time; lots more needed but he is stoutly bred (by dirt standards) and will relish chance to tackle 1m1f first time.
runners.comment: Third in allowance on Tapeta at Turfway Park, staying on; more needed but trip will suit

races.name: Monrovia Stakes Presented By Don Julio (Grade 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Queen Maxima
runners.owner: Dutch Girl Holdings LLC & Irving Ventures LLC

runners.name: Sareeha
runners.owner: Shanderella Stables Cody Cohen & Cole Cohen

races.name: Prix la Force (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)

runners.name: Zambezi
runners.spotlight: Won at Vittel on debut over a mile, as a 2yo, and shown some at least useful form since, the best of which came on his 3yo return in a Listed event at Saint-Cloud (1m, heavy) last month under this rider; proven he's trained on and has race-fitness, so gives the lesser-exposed types something to surpass.
runners.comment: Best run came last time at Saint-Cloud and has a race-fitness edge on some unexposed ones

runners.name: Gusto
runners.spotlight: Comfortably held on debut in December but then won a Chantilly conditions race over a similar trip to today's before a solid third in Listed company at the same venue, when Segall was runner-up; capable of better but this will be his first run on turf, so has to prove he can show same level on grass.
runners.comment: Finished behind Segall last time and yet to race on turf; questions to answer

runners.name: Segall
runners.spotlight: Won at Saint-Cloud on debut (7f, good) before not being able to match the level in two more starts as a 2yo, albeit in much stronger company, one time at this track; finished ahead of Gusto in an AW Listed event at Chantilly (1m1f) but this looks stronger, so more is needed; one of three in the race for trainer Andre Fabre.
runners.comment: Finished ahead of Gusto in an AW Listed event 28 days ago, but will need more today

runners.name: Ebiyar
runners.spotlight: Well-bred sort who made a perfect start to his career over C&D in October on soft ground, showing a fine attitude to win by a narrow margin; holds all the big race entries you'd expect him to have in France, plus one in the Derby, and clearly a fascinating prospect.
runners.comment: Well-bred sort who made a perfect start to his career over C&D in October on soft ground

runners.name: Roi De La Liberte
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up on both starts as a 2yo, once on good ground over a mile the other on soft over the same trip, showing useful form at best; needs to step up on that, though, to be a factor here but every chance he will for leading connections.
runners.comment: Finished runner-up on both starts as a 2yo; needs to step up on that to play a part

runners.name: Zaraki
runners.spotlight: The first foal of a 1m3f winner, who looked a smart prospect when successful here over a mile on very soft ground in August; another who holds all the big-race entries in France and could be anything; interesting.
runners.comment: Looked a smart prospect when successful here over a mile in August; could be anything

races.name: Al Shira'aa Racing Prix Vanteaux (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Vaticana
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on previous efforts when winning Listed event on heavy three weeks ago; worth a try in this higher grade and, although this is tougher, isn't without a chance; no Classic entries.
runners.comment: Turned in best effort when winning Listed event last month; not without a chance

runners.name: Lethal Beauty
runners.spotlight: Has won one of six starts (beat Proxima Du Centaur on AW at Deauville last summer) but below best last time and she'll have to raise her game back on turf.
runners.comment: Has won one of six starts and will have to raise game if she's to win this back on grass

runners.name: Rhiyanna
runners.spotlight: Best effort when notching Group 3 win at Deauville in October; off since but this Diane entry should appreciate the step up to this trip and she'll likely give another good account.
runners.comment: Best effort when notching Group 3 win in October; should run well on reappearance

runners.name: Liberty Amour
runners.spotlight: Won well on AW in the French provinces and may have needed reappearance run on first outing for this trainer last month; Diane entry but more needed if she's to figure.
runners.comment: Won well in the French provinces on third start but well beaten on reappearance; more on
runners.owner: John DAmato Mike Pietrangelo & Mme Francoise Dupuis

runners.name: Proxima Du Centaur
runners.spotlight: Has improved with every start and off the mark on AW at Deauville in November; will likely do better but a bit of a surprise if there's not one or two too good in this company.
runners.comment: Progressive sort who won on AW in November; more needed to follow up in this company

runners.name: Zayida
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on her debut effort when beating a subsequent winner (fourth has also won since) over 1m on soft in a conditions event at Saint-Cloud in autumn; should appreciate this longer trip and this Pouliches/Diane entry has a good chance for her in-form team, who also won this last year.
runners.comment: Useful form to win conditions event at Saint-Cloud; longer trip should suit; good claims

runners.name: Zaraxia
runners.spotlight: Has won both starts, a conditions event over this trip on her debut in September and a Listed event at Marseille Borely; she'll have to make further progress to make it 3-3 but this Pouliches/Diane entry is worth her place in this stronger event.
runners.comment: Has won both starts, including in Listed company, more needed but she can do better

runners.name: Zlata
runners.spotlight: Form of her debut win at Chantilly (1m, soft) in September isn't anything out of the ordinary but she was particularly green and is a Pouliches/Diane entry who represents her top stable; no surprise to see her give a good account.
runners.comment: Form of debut win isn't anything special but no surprise if there's much more to come

runners.name: Concorde Agreement
runners.spotlight: Good impression to run out a wide-margin winner on AW on her debut in December (third won next time); not seen since and remains to be seen how she'll handle these conditions on this reappearance/turf debut but she's a Pouliches/Diane entry who is capable of better.
runners.comment: Created a decent impression when winning on AW on debut; soft ground an unknown

runners.name: Maintain
runners.spotlight: Debut maiden winner over this trip in October but failed to build on that when third in conditions event over 1m on AW the following month; needs to improve but this Pouliches/Diane entry is almost certainly capable of better.
runners.comment: Debut maiden winner last autumn but didn't build on that next time; more needed

races.name: Prix d'Harcourt (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: First Look
runners.spotlight: Proven Group performer, who picked up a career-best whe beating Bay City Roller and Bright Picture in the C&D Prix Dollar (very soft), giving the first named weight-for-age; behind Bright Picture and Azimpour in the Prix Exbury (1m2f, heavy) recently but that was a good effort giving them 4lb and holds sound claims in a stacked field.
runners.comment: Behind Bright Picture and Azimpour recently but was giving both 4lb that day

runners.name: Leffard
runners.spotlight: Unraced as a 2yo but enjoyed quite the season last year, quickly building a smart profile that led him to Group 1 success in the Grand Prix de Paris (1m4f, good to soft), albeit after a down-the-field effort in the  Prix du Jockey Club; behind two on these in the Prix Niel but then ran better than his position suggests in the Arc; likely in for a good season but may want 2f further ideally.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over 1m4f; in for a good year but this trip may not be ideal

runners.name: Bay City Roller
runners.spotlight: Took his form to the next level when landing the Group 1 Grosser Allianz Preis von Bayern (1m4f, soft), but has been running well prior to that, albeit behind both Cualificar and First Look in a couple of races; should cope with the drop in trip but has to give 2lb away to all rivals; Oisin Murphy on for second time.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner when last seen over 1m4f; classy but gives at least 2lb away to all

runners.name: Bright Picture
runners.spotlight: Hasn't stood lots of racing for his age but is top-class when showing his best, which he most notably did when beating superstar gelding Calandagan in a Listed race as a 3yo; was behind both First Look and Bay City Rollder in last year's Prix Dollar, but reversed form with the first named when landing the Prix Exbury (1m2f, heavy) last month from Azimpour; sound chance.
runners.comment: Won the Prix Exbury last time, beating a couple of these; sound chance

runners.name: Azimpour
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt, who has shown plenty of smart form, including when second to Cualificar in the Prix la Force on this card last year before not being too far behind that same rival in the Prix du Jockey Club (1m2f 1110yds, good to soft); made a good return when splitting Bright Picture and First Look in the Prix Exbury (1m2f, heavy) and may be capable of better.
runners.comment: Split Bright Picture and First Look in the recent Prix Exbury; can do better this year

runners.name: Cualificar
runners.spotlight: Won a hot Prix la Force on this card 12 months ago and finished second in the Prix du Jockey Club before confirming his quality when landing the 1m4f Group 2 Prix Niel in September from Bay City Roller off level weights, with Leffard back in sixth; run in the Arc is probably best forgiven and shapes as a big player for this; one of four in the race for Andre Fabre.
runners.comment: Capable of classy form and probably best to forgive his effort in the Arc

runners.name: Oracle
runners.spotlight: Most notable run was when beaten under a length by subsequent Arc winner Daryz in a C&D conditions race last May, when sent off odds-on market leader, but didn't massively build on that effort, and was well beaten following a lengthy break when last seen in November on the AW at Deauville, again beaten at odds-on; trainer/owner have Bright Picture as the clear first string, so hard to see this colt being massively involved.
runners.comment: Quite a bit to find on the figures, with stable/ownermate Bright Picture the 1st string

runners.name: Best Secret
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt, who has won three of his last four starts, the most recent a Listed event at Saint-Cloud in September (1m4f, very soft), but this looks a significant step up for his 4yo return and the owner's retained rider has opted for First Look
runners.comment: Progressive colt last year but a tough task if the principals perform to their best

races.name: Prix Zarkava (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Lemsairbat
runners.owner: H R D McCalmont & Hamish McCalmont & Andrew N C Bengough

runners.name: Desert Spring
runners.owner: Elliott Bloodstock Services William Jackson-Stops & Monaugh
